In rural settings, this means organizing crop shares, communal silos, and the raising of a barn. In urban areas her Parsons often work as civic leaders who manage district funds, negotiate with merchants, solve unemployment issues, or assist the unhoused.

She gets along with Tovah the Father very well, and their clergy tend to overlap in focus. However, while Tovah's priests are extremely focused on building and maintaining structure, Wera's Parsons are always looking ahead at future challenges, trying to solve systemic issues, and looking for small problems right now that could become major catastrophes if they are left unchecked.

Wera finds Ebrugeses far too wasteful of the plenty they embody, and so the two fight constantly. Dremira has a fondness for Wera and often tries to give her esoteric hints about what to do next, but Reaper finds her advice too flighty and insubstantial. For as much as Autumn's Administrator preaches team work and contribution to a group, she often has a difficult time working with others at the same level. She'd much rather be the one in charge of distributing tasks.

Grim Reaper

As summer turns to autumn and the colors of the leaves change, much of the world goes into a state of repose before a harsh winter arrives. During this time, much that was living and vibrant dies out, and so the autumn season has built up an association with death, or at least death's immanent arrival. And so it is that Wera with her hooded cloak, wrapped face, and scythe started to become associated with portents of doom. In parts of the world she is seen as the Grim Reaper, the harvester of souls who collects mortal spirits from Alacar and delivers them to the beyond.

Jata, God of Heroes.name: 贾塔，英雄之神 Jata, God of Heroes
Jata, God of Heroes.text:
勇士

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.CQBx1GPaQDvXDUHL]{Jata}

阵营： LG（LG、NG）

兄弟姐妹：Sewa 与 Sumaworo

神殿：纪念碑、旅店、学院

崇拜者：冒险者、守卫、作者

圣兽：狮子

圣色：黑色与金色

领域：自信、威能、守护、热忱

牧师法术：1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.X9dkmh23lFwMjrYd]{Ant Haul}，4环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.8M03UxGXjYyDFAoy]{Weapon Storm}，8环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.y2cQYLr5mljDSu1G]{Unrelenting Observation}

偏好武器：长剑

神圣能力：力量或魅力

神圣技能：交涉

神圣灌注：医疗

关键典范：以荣誉行事、保护弱者、为所有人的福祉而努力

关键禁忌：作弊、将他人置于险境、煽动混沌

Jata 是每个故事中的英雄，是传奇的救世主，也是拯救整座村庄的谦卑农夫。他是谁、他是什么，会随文化而改变；因此他的名字以及他两位兄弟姐妹的名字，也比均衡的其他神祇更频繁地变化。有些 galtzagorri 将他视作一位性别不定的强大战士，长着蜻蜓般的翅膀，赤裸上身而强壮，屹立不倒。有些 hardriggan 讲述 Sogolon 的故事：一位女战士，生有坚毅的角，说话直来直去，态度干脆利落。无论设定或名字如何变化，勇士都是该文化英雄叙事套路的极致体现。胜算无关紧要，没有任何敌人强到不可战胜。无论挑战为何，他都为正义而战，并且总能笑到最后。

对 Jata 的崇拜形式也如同他的外表一样多变。冒险者最可能在所有职业之上崇拜他，因为他们常将自己的凯旋视作英雄之举，值得吟唱成歌、写入传奇。守卫也敬仰他高贵的事迹、对弱者的保护，以及对击败一切形态邪恶的承诺。勇士乐于将各式各样的任务与机遇赠予最虔诚的信徒，让他们证明自己、成为属于自己的英雄。他可能会伪装成当地店主，请一群冒险者踏上高尚的任务；或以老妇的形态现身，愿意引导那些未来的英雄展开宏大的冒险，去屠杀一条巨龙。

作为“英雄”这一概念本身的化身，Jata 是一位永不安于现状的煽动者。他说服凡人为了更崇高的目标、为了荣耀、为了荣誉而以生命相搏。有些人将他视作惹事者，并非因为他的事迹或他所象征的意义，而是因为他会给他们所爱之人带来危险——那些人可能会被伟大之寄望所动摇。尽管他们乐于被保护，但他们更希望自己的兄弟姐妹与孩子不要跑去当保护者，因为凡人英雄往往活得短暂而暴烈，其人生充满不确定与危险。

然而，反派之神 Sumaworo 总在暗处潜伏。邪恶以多种形式扎根，而由 Jata 来铲除这些邪恶。只要 Sumaworo 仍在腐化凡人的心、将他们引向邪恶，Jata 就会扶持英雄挺身对抗。于是，他们之间的冲突在宏大的循环中延续——一则值得在整个年代被传颂的故事，一次又一次、在时代之中反复讲述。

勇士的信众形形色色，因为英雄主义不只有一种模样。他们可能是刚愎而勇猛的战士，随时准备与邪恶交锋。他们可能是声音轻柔的法师，与自己内心的恶魔搏斗，却仍决意战胜它们、在世上行善。他们也可能是些玩世不恭却心地善良之徒，过于沉迷冒险的战利品，但仍知道何时大义当先，也明白哪怕以自己的生命去拯救他人，也是值得的死法。

计划之外的神祇

当凡人跨越 Alacar 与其他文化接触时，他们关于事件与神话的故事也开始彼此交织。他们讲述克服挑战、戏弄强大敌人，以及沉溺自私或对权力的渴望等故事。从这无数故事中，有三个基础变体从其他故事里脱颖而出：英雄、反派、与浪客。最终，这些变体所承载的能量、对它们的信念，变得如此强大，以至于父母不得不为它们命名并赋予其形态；于是三胞胎诞生：Jata、Sumaworo 与 Sewa。

The Champion

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.CQBx1GPaQDvXDUHL]{Jata}

Alignment: LG (LG, NG)

Siblings: Sewa and Sumaworo

Temples: monuments, inns, academies

Worshippers: adventurers, guards, authors

Sacred Animal: lion

Sacred Colors: black and gold

Domains: confidence, might, protection, zeal

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.X9dkmh23lFwMjrYd]{Ant Haul}, 4th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.8M03UxGXjYyDFAoy]{Weapon Storm}, 8th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.y2cQYLr5mljDSu1G]{Unrelenting Observation}

Favored Weapon: longsword

Divine Ability: Strength or Charisma

Divine Skill: Diplomacy

Divine Font: heal

Key Edicts: act with honor, protect the weak, work for the good of everyone

Key Anathema: cheat, put others in harms' way, foment chaos

Jata is the hero in every story, the legendary savior, and the humble peasant who saves an entire village. Who and what he is changes from one culture to the next, and his name, and that of his two siblings, changes more often than the other deities of the Balance as a result. Some galtzagorri view him as a powerful warrior of indeterminate gender with dragonfly wings, standing strong, shirtless and powerful. Some hardriggan tell tales of Sogolon, a warrior woman with stalwart horns, a direct way of speaking, and no-nonsense attitude. No matter the setting or name, The Champion is the epitome of that culture's heroic tropes. The odds don't matter, and no foe is too strong. Whatever the challenge, he fights the good fight, and always comes out on top.

Worship of Jata comes in as many different forms as his appearance. Adventurers are the most likely to worship him above all other occupations, as they often see their triumphs as heroism, worthy of song and legend. Guards also revere him for his noble deeds, protection of the weak, and commitment to defeating evil in all of its forms. The Champion enjoys gifting his most fervent devotees with all sorts of quests and opportunities to prove themselves and become heroes in their own right. He might disguise himself as a local shopkeeper and ask a group of adventurers to go on a noble quest, or appear as an old woman, willing to guide some would-be heroes on a grand adventure to slay a dragon.

As the embodiment of the very concept of a hero, Jata is an instigator who never stays complacent. He convinces mortals to put their lives on the line for a greater purpose, for glory, and for honor. There are some who see him as a troublemaker, not for his deeds or what he represents, but for the danger he poses to their loved ones who might be swayed by promises of greatness. While they enjoy being protected, they'd rather their siblings and children not run off to do the protecting, for mortal heroes oft lead short and violent lives full of uncertainty and danger.

But Sumaworo, the god of villainy, is always lurking. Evil takes root in many forms, and it is up to Jata to vanquish that evil. For as long as Sumaworo corrupts the hearts of mortals and sways them toward evil, Jata will raise up heroes to stand against them. And so the clash between them continues in a grand cycle, a story worthy to be told throughout the ages, time and time again.

The faithful of The Champion take many forms, for heroism doesn't only look like one thing. They might be headstrong warriors, ready to clash against evil. They might be quiet-voiced mages, struggling with their own inner demons but determined to overcome them and do good in the world. They might be cads with a heart of gold who enjoy the spoils of adventuring a bit too much, but still know when a cause is important and that even laying your own life on the line to save others is a worthy way to die.

Unplanned Deities

As mortals reached out to other cultures across Alacar, their stories of events and mythology also grew intertwined. They'd tell stories of overcoming challenges, tricking powerful foes, and lavishing in selfishness or a thirst for power. From this multitude of stories, three basic archetypes stood out from the others: Hero, Villain, and Rogue. Eventually the energy of these archetypes, the belief in them, grew so potent that The Parents were forced to name them and give them form, thus The Triplets were born: Jata, Sumaworo, and Sewa.

Sumaworo, God of Villains.name: 苏马沃罗，恶徒之神 Sumaworo, God of Villains
Sumaworo, God of Villains.text:
暴君

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.AR3L0HYTRJNfwm3X]{苏玛沃罗}

阵营： LE（LE、NE、CE）

兄弟姐妹：瑟瓦与贾塔

神殿：学院、隐秘之所、官僚机构办公室

崇拜者：政客、军阀、商人

圣兽：蝰蛇

圣色：白色与红色

领域：野心、威能、暴政、秘密

牧师法术：1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.X56VdA98VedAfGTU]{刀刃荆棘丛}，2环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.c2bTWBNO1BYX4Zfg]{误导}，8环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.8AMvNVOUEtxBCDvJ]{巨怪形态}

偏好武器：匕首

神圣能力：体质或智力

神圣技能：欺瞒

神圣灌注：伤害

关键典范：夺取你应得之物，先照顾好自己，用任何手段去触及你渴望的结局

关键禁忌：做事不图个人利益，否认自身欲望，让自己在破坏规则时被抓个正着

每个人都有欲望。每个人都想要某样东西、需要某样东西、相信某样东西能让自己的人生圆满。苏玛沃罗以他们灵魂中这颗“需要”的种子为食，令其滋长、使其茁壮、精心培育，直到那个人相信：满足这种需要，是唯一重要的事。

他代表每个故事里的反派，也是将邪恶欲望赋予神性形态的存在，但他并不会仅仅为了作恶而故作姿态。相反，他会问自己：“何为邪恶？”在苏玛沃罗看来，“邪恶”这一概念不过是那些自诩“善良”者为了能以自命正义之名攻击一切敌人而发明的借口。“善良”只是想将自己的意志强加于“邪恶”之上，并以此作为理由，用暴力摧毁对手。被认定为“邪恶”的，不过是一群竭尽全力去实现目标的人——无论那些目标是什么。他们唯一的“罪”是想要某样东西、为之奋斗、追求自己的幸福。这怎么能被称为邪恶？当与凡人互动时，暴君（尽管那是自命正义者强加给他的不公称号）会在他们耳畔低语，提醒他们自身的渴求、那些他们应得的渴望之物，以及获得它的计划。对苏玛沃罗的崇拜往往是缓慢发生的：人们渐渐被欲望吞噬，而苏玛沃罗与他的神职人员则承诺一条通往满足之路。

偶尔，一大群人也会为了共同目标而迅速聚集到苏玛沃罗身边。例如，他们可能想根除某个“外来者”群体——他们觉得对方夺走了本该属于自己的资源，并改变了他们的生活方式。暴君将他们的恐惧培育成仇恨，并将其武器化，对准替罪羊。他能把他们煽动成一群同仇敌忾的狂热暴民，向他们灌输谎言，并把自己的虚假之词辩解为另一种观点，甚至是唯一正确的观点；而其他所谓“事实”则是错误的，乃至恶意捏造的。毕竟，若他们已坚信某个信念，又找到了支持该信念的证据，又何必质询它？苏玛沃罗告诉他们要紧紧抓住它，并用尽全力大声叫嚷那些证据！他们应当知道自己的信念理直气壮，并应当清洗一切持异议者。

归根结底，苏玛沃罗教导他的追随者：真正重要的只有他们自己。为何要为他人牺牲任何东西？为他人奉献自身，对他们有什么好处？那样怎么可能赢？他们赢不了，因此先照顾好自己才是自然之道。依暴君所言，崇拜苏玛沃罗并不邪恶——那只是被误导的人说的话。他们不明白，苏玛沃罗是在引导人们走向完美之路。一旦某人拥有了自己渴望的一切，那么他们也能帮助他人得到想要的东西；但他们必须把自己放在第一位，否则又能有何用？

欲望之“罪”

想要。每个类人生物都有欲望、有渴求、有需要。有时，这些需要如此迫切，却又遥不可及，以至于他们会去做越来越可疑的事情来得到自己想要的东西。他们不把自己视为恶棍，而是环境的受害者。“我不得不这么做”和“别无他法！”是绝望者的呼喊——他们被逼到去做不可言说之事。它往往开始得很缓慢，随后逐渐累积，因为他们想要的东西、他们应得的东西……就近在咫尺却又触不可及。于是他们伸得更远，为了够到它而踩踏他人。毕竟，他们的需要合情合理，而不择手段去得到它也不过是自然之举。

The Tyrant

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.AR3L0HYTRJNfwm3X]{Sumaworo}

Alignment: LE (LE, NE, CE)

Siblings: Sewa and Jata

Temples: academies, secret places, bureaucratic offices

Worshippers: politicians, warlords, merchants

Sacred Animal: viper

Sacred Colors: white and red

Domains: ambition, might, tyranny, secrets

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.X56VdA98VedAfGTU]{Thicket of Knives}, 2nd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.c2bTWBNO1BYX4Zfg]{Misdirection}, 8th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.8AMvNVOUEtxBCDvJ]{Monstrosity Form}

Favored Weapon: dagger

Divine Ability: Constitution or Intelligence

Divine Skill: Deception

Divine Font: harm

Key Edicts: take what you deserve, take care of yourself first, use any means to reach your desired ends

Key Anathema: act without personal gain, deny yourself your desires, get caught breaking the rules

Everyone has a desire. Everyone has something they want, something that they need, something they believe will make their lives complete. Sumaworo feeds on this seed of need within their soul, grows it, and nurtures it, until the person believes that fulfilling that need is the only thing that matters.

While he represents the villain in every story, and is made of wicked desire given godly form, he doesn't simply twirl his mustache just for the evil of it. Rather, he asks himself the question, "What is evil?" To Sumaworo, the concept of "evil" is merely a justification those who label themselves as "good" invented in order to allow them to attack all their enemies in the name of self-righteousness. The "good" just want to impose their own will on the "evil" and use this as justification to violently ruin their opponents. Those identified as "evil" are simply people trying their hardest to achieve their goals, whatever those might be. Their only sins are to want something, to strive for it, and to pursue their own happiness. How can that be called evil? When interacting with mortals, The Tyrant (though that is an unjust title, given to him by the self-righteous), whispers in their ears to remind them of their own wants, the things they crave that they deserve, and a plan for how to get it. The worship of Sumaworo often happens slowly as people become consumed with their desires while Sumaworo and his clergy promise a path to satisfaction.

Occasionally a large group of people will come together to Sumaworo's side quickly in pursuit of a common goal. For example, they might wish to eradicate an outgroup of "others" that they feel is taking away resources meant for them and altering their way of life. The Tyrant cultivates their fear into hatred and weaponizes it against the scapegoat. He's able to whip them into a frenzied mob of like-minded folk, feeding them lies and justifying his falsehoods as an alternative point of view, or perhaps the only correct point of view, while the other supposed "facts" are incorrect or even maliciously false. After all, if they have a firmly held belief and find evidence to support that belief, what need is there to interrogate it? Sumaworo tells them to hold on to it and shout that evidence at the top of their lungs! They should know that they are justified in their beliefs and should purge all who disagree.

At the end of the day, Sumaworo teaches that his followers are the only one who really matters. Why should they sacrifice anything for others? What good is it to them to give of themself for another? How can they win that way? They can't, and so looking out for themself first is only natural. To worship Sumaworo isn't evil, according to the Tyrant, that's just something misguided people say. They don't understand that Sumaworo helps guide people on the path to perfection. Once someone has everything they desire, then they can help others get what they want too, but they have to put themself first or what good will they be?

The “Sin” of Desire

To want. Every humanoid has desire, has cravings, has needs. Sometimes these needs are so urgent, and yet so out of reach, that they do ever-increasingly dubious things to get what they want. They don't see themselves as villains, but as victims of circumstance. "I had to" and "There was no other way!" are the cries of the desperate, driven to do unspeakable things. It often starts out slowly and builds because the thing they want, the thing they deserve... lies just out of reach. So they reach further, and step on others to get there. After all, their need is justified and doing whatever they need to do to get it is only natural.

Sewa, God of Rogues.name: 瑟瓦，盗贼之神 Sewa, God of Rogues
Sewa, God of Rogues.text:
愚者

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.A4FTvDHeGNWguNyO]{Sewa}

阵营： CN（NG，CG，N，CN，NE，CE）

手足：Jata 与 Sumaworo

神殿：旅店、赌博厅、盗贼公会

崇拜者：赌徒、盗贼、间谍、表演者

圣兽：蜘蛛

圣色：灰色与绿色

领域：自由、幸运、魔法、诡计

牧师法术：1环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.i35dpZFI7jZcRoBo]{Illusory Disguise}，2环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.XXqE1eY3w3z6xJCB]{Invisibility}，5环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.BoA00y45uDlmou07]{Secret Chest}

偏好武器：细剑

神圣能力：敏捷或感知

神圣技能：隐匿

神圣圣池：伤害或治疗

关键典范：享受乐趣、让人发笑、胡闹一番然后看看会发生什么

关键禁忌：立下庄严誓言、把事情看得过于严肃、拒绝沉溺于你的好奇心

从把君王变形成别的样子，到送来一场倾盆风暴淹没干旱的土地，Sewa 总是在凡人之间制造混乱。她的恶作剧通常具有破坏性，偶尔也很残忍，但永远都是她自己觉得无比好笑的事。若有人在飓风里笑得出来，那多半就是 Sewa。她有许多头衔与人格面具，随心所欲地在其间切换。她是愚者、无赖、弄臣、间谍、妖姬。她会采取最契合自己心情与某次行动目标的任何形态与人设。

她的追随者对生活也往往抱持同样轻松的态度，不过他们采取的手段比无赖本人要更不极端、也不那么混沌。许多丑角巨龙与各式各样的喜剧表演者都爱慕 Sewa，并把她视作缪斯；而盗贼与间谍则欣赏她作为暗影中的工作者、命运的操弄者这一真实本性。她的虔信者常常像她一样运用魔法，以达成各自的目标。他们把魔法视为被赋予目的的混沌之精髓——哪怕那个目的仅仅是创造一个幻象，用以将他们其他意图掩盖在世人眼前。

Sewa 很少直接与她的追随者互动，尽管她在很久以前曾经常这么做。这是因为她发现自己每次现身，她的诡计魔法就难免会给那位追随者带来某种麻烦；而这种麻烦又总会惹人厌地让对方突然失去幽默感，转而谴责她。凭借艰难得来的经验，她明白了：让追随者自行追求他们自己的滑稽风格，比起在暗中对他们事无巨细地操控，更能把喜悦与欢笑散播到世界各处。以身作则对 Sewa 来说是更好的策略——这样她既能尽情取乐，又不会逼得那些真的喜欢她的人改去追随无聊而乏味的东西，比如贞洁、职责与荣誉。

偶尔，Sewa 的手足会选择把她拉来协助某个阴谋、英雄任务或诸如此类的事情，尽管她总会不可避免地按自己的方式“加工”一切。她的兄弟 Jata 与 Sumaworo 一直在相互争斗。典型情况下，先是其中一方做了什么惹恼另一方的事，然后两人就开始在新的冲突中彼此较劲，试图“赢下”这场纷争。往往，战况落入下风的那一方会把 Sewa 当作最后手段拉来援助；而她当然会去帮助她亲爱的兄弟。另一位兄弟通常会心生嫉妒，并把她当作不可信的骗术师而加以谴责。

然而，两位兄弟往往都意识不到：从一开始，整场冲突就是 Sewa 策划的。她可能会从他们的争斗中得到些什么：一个聚落里的混沌、一头新的搞笑怪物在外游荡、或是让某位领袖或宗教人物做出蠢事并毁掉全部声誉。有一次，她让两人都相信对方将会在一段被遗忘的海滩狭长地带找到一件足以改变世界的神祇宝藏。当他们为字面意义上的沙粒争得不可开交时，她却在附近把他们两方的教团都转化为对她的崇拜者。

胡闹

Sewa 的诸多恶作剧曾引发战争、促成和平条约、导致退位与绑架。许多冒险任务的开端，都源于愚者那扭曲玩笑所引发的烂摊子需要冒险者来收拾。若你觉得游戏里的事件开始变得有些沉闷，不妨考虑让玩家角色迎来一次 Sewa 的造访，或遇到她留下些什么的余波，以便搅动局势、增添变数。

The Fool

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.A4FTvDHeGNWguNyO]{Sewa}

Alignment: CN (NG, CG, N, CN, NE, CE)

Siblings: Jata and Sumaworo

Temples: inns, gambling halls, thieves' guilds

Worshippers: gamblers, thieves, spies, performers

Sacred Animal: spider

Sacred Colors: gray and green

Domains: freedom, luck, magic, trickery

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.i35dpZFI7jZcRoBo]{Illusory Disguise}, 2nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.XXqE1eY3w3z6xJCB]{Invisibility}, 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.BoA00y45uDlmou07]{Secret Chest}

Favored Weapon: rapier

Divine Ability: Dexterity or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Stealth

Divine Font: harm or heal

Key Edicts: have fun, make people laugh, mess around and find out

Key Anathema: take a solemn vow, take things too seriously, refuse to indulge your curiosity

From polymorphing monarchs to sending a torrential storm to flood parched lands, Sewa is constantly wreaking havoc on mortals. Her japes are usually disruptive and occasionally cruel, but always things she finds hilarious. If there's someone laughing in a hurricane, it's probably Sewa. She has many titles and personas she uses, switching between them at a whim. She is The Fool, The Rogue, The Jester, The Spy, and The Enchantress. She'll take whatever form and persona best fit her mood and her goals for a particular endeavor.

Her followers tend to have equally light-hearted approaches to life, but while taking less extreme and chaotic measures than the Rogue herself. Many harlequin dragons and comedic performers of all sorts adore Sewa and consider her their muse, while thieves and spies enjoy her true nature as a worker of the shadows and manipulator of fates. Her faithful often employ magic, just as she does, in order to achieve their various goals. They see magic as the essence of chaos given purpose, even if that purpose is only to create an illusion with which to mask their other intentions from the world.

Sewa rarely interacts with her followers directly, even though she used to do so once upon a time. This is because she finds that every time she does so, her trickster magic inevitably causes the follower some sort of difficulty, which has an annoying tendency to cause them to suddenly lose their sense of humor and denounce her. From hard-earned experience, she's learned that leaving her followers alone to pursue their own style of hilarity is a much better way to spread joy and mirth throughout the world than micromanaging them from the shadows. Leading by example has been a much better tactic for Sewa, as she then gets to have her fun without causing people who actually like her to turn to boring and banal things like chastity, duty, and honor.

Occasionally Sewa's siblings make the choice to pull her in to assist on some scheme or heroic quest or something, despite the fact that she inevitably puts her own spin on things. Her brothers, Jata and Sumaworo, are constantly fighting with each other. In a typical scenario, first one of them does something to upset the other, then the two start one-upping each other in their attempts to "win" whatever the new conflict is. Often whichever of the two of them is losing the fight decides to enlist Sewa to aid them as a last resort, and of course she comes to her darling brother's aid. The other brother is usually jealous and denounces her for the untrustworthy trickster that she is.

However, neither brother tends to realize when Sewa is the one who orchestrated the entire conflict in the first place. She might get something out of their fighting: chaos in a settlement, a new hilarious monster on the loose, or getting a leader or religious figure to do something foolish and ruin their entire reputation. Once, she convinced both of them that the other was going to find a world-altering treasure of the gods on a forgotten strip of beach. While they fought over literal grains of sand, she was out converting both of their nearby cults to worship her instead.

Shenanigans

The many japes of Sewa have caused wars, peace treaties, abdications, and abductions. Many quests begin when one of The Fool's twisted jokes require adventurers to handle the fallout. If events are getting a bit dreary in your game, consider having the player characters receive a visit from Sewa or some of her aftermath in order to mix things up.

Faiths of the Isles.name: 群岛信仰 Faiths of the Isles
Faiths of the Isles.text:
由长者黛娜讲述给森巴拉的孩子们听。

靛蓝群岛是阿拉卡尔世界各地众多信仰的家园，其中最著名的是被称为“艾尔德”和“平衡”的宗教。“艾尔德”是一种古老的元素宗教，据人们所知自时间黎明起便已存在，强调冥想与自我完善，几乎不在意传教。然而，“平衡”却是一种极其流行的宗教，最初对阿拉卡尔而言是外来入侵的。当两位强大的跨维度存在来到生机勃勃的阿拉卡尔世界时，他们对这里产生了喜爱，并开始创造神祇后裔，以实现其目标，并将世界的环境改变为合其心意的样子。每当他们遭遇新的挫折，便会作为回应诞下新的孩子；很快，世界便充满了他们的新万神殿。他们将其称为“平衡”，其名源自这些后裔的各个支系彼此制衡，从而在世界的某一性相上维持均衡——无论是天气、时代，还是生命的循环。

神祇的常用术语

以下术语用于阿拉卡尔，用以描述不同的宗教与神祇团体。尽管这些术语主要与“平衡”相关，但也同样指涉若干其他宗教。

家族 “平衡”的神祇大多出自同一家族，由“母亲”与“父亲”以成对或成组的方式诞下子嗣，使其在宇宙层面彼此平衡。他们合称为“家族”。

双亲 “双亲”一词特指托娃——父亲——与莉琳——母亲——这两位最初抵达阿拉卡尔的强大跨维度存在；他们后来诞下塔利尔，并建立了“平衡”。

手足 每一组由“双亲”诞生的神祇都被视为“手足”——彼此相关、关系最为亲近的一对或一组神祇。尽管所有“子女”（由父亲与母亲诞生的神祇）彼此都是兄弟姐妹，但“手足”一词通常指一组同时诞生、作为一套彼此制衡的神祇。本文所述的手足团体是靛蓝群岛最受欢迎的几组，但并非所有手足的完整清单。

子女 所有“手足”合称为“子女”。尽管从技术上说，他们比父亲与母亲的力量更弱，但每一位“子女”往往在塑造与治理阿拉卡尔方面扮演更积极的角色。虽然巨龙神祇塔利尔是“双亲”的第一个孩子，但他们不属于任何一组“手足”，且被困在月亮塔利诺斯之内，因此在提及“子女”时常将其排除在外。

平衡 “平衡”既是崇拜“双亲”及其“子女”的宗教通称，也是指一种整体体系：其中每一组“手足”彼此牵制，以确保不会重演塔利尔之事。

艾尔德 阿拉卡尔最古老的宗教之一，其年代远早于“平衡”。“艾尔德”教导人们崇拜二十种元素能量，以及与这些能量相关的生命素、灵体、思念形态与其他存在。

谜团 这些强大的实体有时会从阿拉卡尔世界之外低语，直接呢喃进凡人的心里。他们的意图异质而令人不安，且往往会破坏世界的和谐。即便如此，凡人仍有时会为了力量、钱、爱情，或他们在灵魂深处隐藏的任何卑劣冲动而服从这些低语。来自彼岸的低语知晓这些冲动，以此为食，并在阿拉卡尔上建立影响力。在所有“子女”之中，只有库乌及其信徒似乎能较为经常地铲除这些“谜团”的影响。

其他神祇 阿拉卡尔还有许多其他信仰，其中一些拥有自己的神祇。他们通常不如“子女”那般积极介入，但由于他们存在于“平衡”之外，反而更容易扰乱它。在这些其他神祇之中，强大的天界、监察者与邪魔——例如恶魔领主——尤其可能这么做。

无神祇的信仰 崇敬神祇的宗教（如“平衡”）或崇拜某一核心概念的众多分散显现（如“艾尔德”）是群岛居民最熟悉的类型。然而，世界各地还存在更为深奥的宗教，信念迥异；其中包括妖精族裔“希德”所持的信仰：他们相信“命运”以神祇力量的元叙事形式存在于世界中，牵引他们的故事走向熟悉的套路与引人入胜的结局。

什么是神祇？

“平衡”的神祇皆为父亲与母亲的后裔——两位强大的跨维度存在从别处来到阿拉卡尔；然而关于那个“别处”究竟在哪里，各方资料说法不一。他们栖居于阿拉卡尔世界本身，而非生活在某个其他位面的存在之地，但这并不意味着他们就与共享此世的阿拉卡尔凡人生物相同。

从本质上看，这些神祇是多面向、多维度的存在，超越了族裔与性别等概念；但为了与阿拉卡尔的凡人存在建立联系，他们会披上这两种社会建构作为外观。因此，任何人看到一位“平衡”的神祇时，都会将其视为自己心目中该神祇的理想形象，几乎总是呈现为观察者自身族裔的一员。这意味着若多人同时注视同一位神祇，他们将各自看到不同的样貌，除非神祇刻意通过采用一致的伪装来隐藏其神性与身份。要在瑟娃又重施故技时发现她，可没那么容易！

此外，这些神祇同时拥有所有性别。他们并不局限于单一性别，而是泛性别，会随时代在不同性别之间变换。在任意一段时间伸展内——往往跨越多个凡人世代——他们会倾向于一种自己认为最适合的性别表达。此处所使用的性别和代称，是这些神祇在当前时代于阿拉卡尔最常采用的；但即便如此，当情境需要时，也常能见到他们以不同性别出现。

这种性别流动性甚至也适用于父亲与母亲，尽管他们的称号带有性别意味；不过他们似乎是“平衡”之中唯二对当前性别有偏好的神祇。在他们性别反转或不同的时期，父亲有时会使用另一个称号，例如“女族长”；而母亲有时会使用另一个称号，例如“始祖”。

由于“平衡”的运作方式，各组“手足”往往会一同将倾向转向某一特定性别。例如，阿蒙与卡斯蒂利，以及“时代手足”，几乎总是性别一致；因此若阿蒙以女性或非二元性别出现，卡斯蒂利也会如此。还有其他一些“手足”组合几乎总是呈现相反性别，以及其他更复杂的模式，用以决定这些神祇在千年流转中所采取身份的细节。

正是他们积极干预世界、以凡人般的样貌现身以与我们相连，使“平衡”的神祇如此受欢迎。办个派对，阿蒙可能会不请自来。若你向穆鲁伊献祭得足够频繁，他或许会帮你碾碎闯入者。

As told by Elder Dyna to the children of Sembala.

The Indigo Isles are home to many of the faiths present across the world of Alacar, most notably the religions known as the Eld and the Balance. The Eld is an ancient elemental religion that has been present, as far as anyone knows, since the dawn of time, with a focus on meditation and self-improvement and little care for proselytizing. The Balance, however, is a wildly popular religion, originally invasive to Alacar. When two powerful interdimensional beings traveled to the vibrant world of Alacar, they took a liking to it and began to create deific progeny in order to enact goals and change the world's circumstances to their liking. When they reached new setbacks, they birthed new children in response, and soon the world was full of their new pantheon, which they called the Balance, based on the way that the sets of descendants balanced each other out in order to maintain an equilibrium in one aspect of the world, whether it be weather, time, or the cycle of life.

Common Terms Of The Deities

The following terms are used on Alacar to describe various religions and groups of deities. Though these terms are primarily those associated with the Balance, they reference several other religions as well.

The Family Deities of the Balance are largely from the same family, with the Mother and the Father having children in pairs or groups who are cosmologically balanced with each other. All of them collectively are known as the Family.

Parents The term "the Parents" refers specifically to Tova, the Father, and Lilin, the Mother, the original two powerful interdimensional beings who arrived on Alacar, later birthed Talir, and then established the Balance.

Siblings Each group of deities born from The Parents are considered Siblings, a pair or a group of deities who are related and have the closest relationships with each other. Though all of the Children (deities born from the Father and the Mother) are siblings of each other, the term "Siblings" typically refers to a group of deities born together as a set and balancing each other out. The groups of Siblings in this article are some of the most popular in the Indigo Isles, but they aren't the complete list of all Siblings.

Children All of the Siblings collectively are referred to as "the Children. Though technically less powerful than the Father and the Mother, each of the Children tends to take a more active role in shaping and governing Alacar. While the dragon deity Talir was the first child of the Parents, they aren't part of a group of Siblings, and they're trapped inside the moon Talinos, so it's common to exclude them when referring to the Children.

The Balance The Balance is both the common name for the religion worshiping The Parents and their Children as well as the name for the overall system by which each group of Siblings keeps each other in check and ensures that there isn't a repeat of what happened with Talir.

The Eld One of Alacar's oldest religions, significantly predating the Balance, the Eld teaches worship of twenty elemental energies as well as the vitae, spirits, thoughtforms, and other beings associated with those energies.

Enigmas These powerful entities sometimes whisper from beyond the world of Alacar and directly into the hearts of mortals. Their intentions are alien, disturbing, and often destructive to the harmony of the world. Nevertheless, mortals sometimes obey these whispers for power, money, love, or whatever vile urges they hide deep in their souls. The whispers from beyond know of these urges, feed on them, and build influence over Alacar. Out of all the Children, only Kuu and their faithful seem to be able to root out the enigmas' influence with any regularity.

Other Deities There are many other faiths in Alacar, some of which have their own deities. They are generally less involved than the Children, but because they exist outside of the Balance, can disrupt it more easily. Among these other deities, powerful celestials, monitors, and fiends such as demon lords are especially likely to do so.

Faiths Without Deities Religions that venerate deities, such as the Balance, or that worship many disparate manifestations of a central concept, like the Eld, are the sorts most familiar to inhabitants of the Isles. However, even more esoteric religions exist across the world with extremely different beliefs, including the belief among the fey ancestry known as sidhe that Fate exists in the world as a metanarrative of deific power, tugging their stories toward familiar tropes and interesting conclusions.

What Is A Deity?

The deities of the Balance are all descended from the Father and the Mother, two powerful interdimensional beings who came to Alacar from elsewhere, though sources conflict when it comes to determining where that "elsewhere" may be. They inhabit the world of Alacar itself rather than living on some other plane of existence, but that doesn't mean that they are quite like the mortal creatures of Alacar who share the world with them.

At their core, these deities are multifaceted, many- dimensional beings that transcend concepts like ancestry and gender, but they don both of these societal constructs as guises in order to relate to the mortal beings of Alacar. As a result, anyone who sees one of the deities of the Balance sees them as their own ideal image of that deity, almost always as a member of the viewer's own ancestry. This means that if several people are looking at a deity, they will all see something different, unless the deity is intentionally seeking to hide their divinity and identity by adopting a consistent disguise. It's not that easy to spot Sewa when she's up to her old tricks again!

Furthermore, the deities are of all genders at once. They aren't limited to a single gender but are instead pangender, shifting between genders over time. During any given stretch of time, often spanning multiple mortal generations, they tend towards a particular gender expression that they feel best suits them. The genders and pronouns expressed here are the ones the deities most often use in Alacar at this time, but even then, they can often be found in a different gender when it suits the occasion.

This gender fluidity even applies to the Father and the Mother, despite their gendered titles, though they are the only deities in all of the Balance that seem to have a bias towards their current genders. In times when their genders are reversed or different, the Father sometimes takes another title, such as The Matriarch and the Mother sometimes takes another title, such as The Progenitor.

Due to the ways of the Balance, groups of Siblings tend to shift their tendencies towards a particular gender together. For instance, Amon and Castilli, and the Siblings of Time, almost always match genders, so if Amon appears as female or nonbinary, Castilli would too. There are other pairs of Siblings that almost always have opposing genders and other, more complicated patterns that determine the details of these deities' assumed identities across the millennia.

It's their active intervention into the world, appearing like mortals in order to relate to us, that makes the deities of the Balance so popular. Throw a party, and Amon might crash it. Make frequent enough offerings to Mului, and he might help you crush interlopers.

The Balance Pantheons.name: 平衡万神殿 The Balance Pantheons
The Balance Pantheons.text:
The following pantheons are available to worshippers of the Balance. Unlike most pantheons, worshippers of the Balance don't need to pick a specific patron deity when selecting one of these pantheons, as they are meant to worship all of them equally and keep things balanced. This also means that most of the Balance pantheons are true neutral, except for a few pantheons based on a set of Siblings with unbalanced alignments.

The Parents

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.9vyVv681gh5ehX0k]{The Parents}

Pantheon Members: Lilin, Tova

Alignment: N (NG, N, NE)

Domains: confidence, creation, family, knowledge

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xqmHD8JIjak15lRk]{Unseen Servant}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.TUj8eugNqAvB1vVR]{Creation}, 9th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KPDHmmjJiw7PhTYF]{Resplendent Mansion}

Favored Weapon: bola

Divine Ability: Intelligence or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Society

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: find balance between tradition and innovation, create something that lasts, find compromises to contentious problems

Key Anathema: stubbornly entrench and refuse to compromise, tear down without any plan to rebuild, tip the scales in your own favor

Tova, the Father, and Lilin, the Mother, are the original cosmic voyagers who found the world of Alacar in a battle for survival against a horde of kaiju. While Tova brings strict order and laws and Lilin incites chaos and change, together they build and create new things. The pantheon of the Parents (which sometimes puts on airs and calls itself the pantheon of the Balance as a whole) worships the two in equal measure, leading to priests who don't fit in easily with either Tova's or Lilin's own.

Life and Death

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.d3cvOcWdxPeadDhR]{Life and Death}

Pantheon Members: Aoz, Zoa

Alignment: N (NG, N, NE)

Domains: death, dreams, fate, healing

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4c1c6eNzU1PFGkAy]{Endure}, 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.AMEu5zzLN7uCX645]{Ghostly Weapon}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ht35SDf9PDStJfoC]{Spirit Song}

Favored Weapon: chakram

Divine Ability: Constitution or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Medicine

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: destroy undead, help create life, respect the dead and their resting place

Key Anathema: create undead, prevent life from flourishing, return the dead to life

Aoz and Zoa, the first pair of Siblings, help shepherd the process of life and death, and pantheists of Life and Death worship both halves of the process equally, seeing everything as a cycle, with either life or death meaningless without the other.

War and Peace

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.y4mzzx7Sw5KBWXJJ]{War and Peace}

Pantheon Members: Semelleh, Zatera

Alignment: N (LN, N, CN)

Domains: might, perfection, protection, zeal

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.jsWthW1Qy6tg3SwD]{Draw Ire}, 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WsUwpfmhKrKwoIe3]{Slow}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Oj1PJBMQD9vuwCv7]{Maze}

Favored Weapon: dagger

Divine Ability: Constitution or Charisma

Divine Skill: Diplomacy or Intimidation

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: fight to bring peace, build alliances for mutual protection and victory, compromise between aggression and passivity

Key Anathema: break truce or alliance, start an unjust or unwise war, back down from a fight when doing so will lead to more bloodshed

Zatera and Semelleh represent both sides of interpersonal and interstate relationships. Followers of the pantheon of War and Peace keep balance between the two, using warfare to increase stability and foster peace while creating peaceful alliances to improve strength in war.

Revelry and Restraint

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.McWmJZCE5Vw5FdTD]{Revelry and Restraint}

Pantheon Members: Amon, Castilli

Alignment: N (NG, N, NE)

Domains: confidence, freedom, indulgence, truth

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.pBevG6bSQOiyflev]{Befuddle}, 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.DCQHaLrYXMI37dvW]{Paralyze}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.GP3wewkQXEPrLxYj]{Subconscious Suggestion}

Favored Weapon: meteor hammer

Divine Ability: Wisdom or Charisma

Divine Skill: Society

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: work diligently so you can relax later, teach others discipline and show them how to let loose, stay calm in a crisis and exult when you can

Key Anathema: slack off on the job, overwork yourself into burnout, intentionally ruin the mood (whether of dignity or celebration)

Amon and Castilli travel together and keep each other from veering to extremes. Followers of the pantheon of Revelry and Restraint know when to be serious and work diligently and when to let loose and play hard.

Feast and Famine

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.HcuTVcMylGs7CZHe]{Feast and Famine}

Pantheon Members: Ebrugeses, Havath

Alignment: CN (N, CN, CE)

Domains: ambition, indulgence, trickery, wealth

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.qTr2oCgIXl703Whb]{Thoughtful Gift}, 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.F9mA2Bg27QKniIdv]{Bottomless Stomach}, 6th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}

Favored Weapon: greatclub

Divine Ability: Wisdom or Charisma

Divine Skill: Survival

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: eat well now but save some for lean times, share with others within your means, cherish what you have: tomorrow it could be gone

Key Anathema: focus too much on the now at the expense of the future, waste resources needlessly, lose track of what you have

Ebrugeses and Havath are the Siblings of plenty and scarcity, and there are two conceivable ways to combine their teachings into a pantheon. The Balance pantheon of Feast and Famine does so in a way that attempts to balance excess and famine and find a middle path, but rumors exist that Havath encourages an unbalanced combination that recommends overindulging in order to create a famine, like locusts devouring everything in their path. If a second pantheon of Indulgent Loss exists, it operates in secret and isn't accepted as part of the Balance; perhaps it's a cult of Havath masquerading as a second pantheon.

The Primordial Energies

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.JFs0yEUfbkMXphBC]{The Primordial Energies}

Pantheon Members: Baoyan, Kuu, Mului, Than Dat, Thongfa

Alignment: N (NG, LN, N, CN, NE)

Domains: air, earth, fire, void, water

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.MVrxZarUTnJxAUN8]{Tether}, 2nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.kMffb6SvhCBMMI4k]{Elemental Zone}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.e36Z2t6tLdW3RUzZ]{Elemental Gift}

Favored Weapon: shield boss

Divine Ability: Strength or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Nature

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: employ each of the primordial energies when they have the most potential, use your power to bind threats and protect others, learn to be in tune with the energy of the universe

Key Anathema: wield power for its own sake, become reliant on one type of energy or approach, release that which should remain sealed

The five Siblings of primordial energy were born to imprison Talir, the primordial dragon deity and firstborn of the Parents. Worshippers of the Primordial Energies as a pantheon respect the energies as a whole, especially their role in keeping Talir sealed.

The Siblings of Time

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.1bKZoRyroRdq8u2L]{The Siblings of Time}

Pantheon Members: Awanna, Dremira, Herst

Alignment: N (LG, NG, CG, LN, N, CN)

Domains: fate, knowledge, time, vigil

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aEM2cttJ2eYcLssW]{Fleet Step}, 3nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.MNiT0dHol5fEcKlz]{Threefold Aspect}, 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.OdqM06M0wDUqZWiR]{Cast into Time}

Favored Weapon: sap

Divine Ability: Wisdom or Charisma

Divine Skill: Occultism

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: protect the integrity of the timeline, follow oracular messages from the Siblings of Time, put into practice in the present what you learned from the past to lead to a better future

Key Anathema: use time magic to go back in time and change events, disobey or question orders from the Siblings of Time, repeat your mistakes of the past in the future without learning from them

Herst, Awanna, and Dremira, the three Siblings of Time (currently appearing most often in feminine forms as the three Sisters of Time), guard against abuse of time travel and attempt to guide the timeline to better possibilities. Mortal worshippers of the Siblings of Time as a pantheon often take a similar stance, policing the timeline as their three deities do. The Siblings of Time trust followers of the pantheon somewhat more than other mortals and even at times more than their own individual worshippers, and they sometimes collectively send these pantheists oracular messages or quests to fulfill.

The Four Seasons

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.M1IDPTVxbkQiiHtz]{The Four Seasons}

Pantheon Members: Chakisa, Chira, Kasa, Wera

Alignment: N (N)

Domains: duty, family, moon, sun

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KcLVELhCUcKXxiKE]{Tailwind}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.D31YX7zvRBvenTAz]{Petal Storm}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.A2JfEKe6BZcTG1S8]{Fly}

Favored Weapon: fighting fan

Divine Ability: Strength or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Survival

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: work hard to prepare for the worst, help others find harmony and relationships, enjoy the differences that come from the changing of the seasons

Key Anathema: shirk work and responsibility, cause strife in others' relationships, let your impatience lead you into danger

Kasa, Chakisa, Wera, and Chira make up the four seasons that cycle across the world of Alacar, as well as four extremely different outlooks on life. Worshippers of the Four Seasons as a pantheon respect the cycle itself as sacred and focus on the lessons learned from balancing these various lifestyles. After all, even Chakisa and Kasa get along extremely well, despite the fact that they are (literally, in Kasa's case) polar opposites.

The Three Stories

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.H4cRieUhzeDoyuT5]{The Three Stories}

Pantheon Members: Jata, Sewa, Sumaworo

Alignment: N (LN, N, CN)

Domains: creation, fate, knowledge, trickery

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.TjWHgXqPv5jywMti]{Pocket Library}, 2nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.j8vIoIEWElvpwkcI]{Mirror Image}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y2cQYLr5mljDSu1G]{Unrelenting Observation}

Favored Weapon: staff

Divine Ability: Intelligence or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Society

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal}

Key Edicts: learn lessons and wisdom from stories, tell and write new tales, proclaim a hero or trickster or villain you find

Key Anathema: ignore the signs of imminent ruin or danger in your own story or those of others, destroy or derail a burgeoning story before it can reach its climax, disrespect or step out of your own role in a story

Jata, Sewa, and Sumaworo, three of the greatest stories of mortalkind, form the only unplanned Siblings of the Balance, the Three Stories. Mortals who follow this pantheon tend to be storytellers and authors with beliefs similar to the sidhe worship of the metanarrative Fate as a concept (see Battlezoo Ancestries Sidhe for more details).

The Eld.name: 长老 The Eld
The Eld.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.Lp5PLsGqdfRcekMM]{Tairopoof}

泰洛噗（Tairopoof），通常被称为“空气蝙蝠艾达萌”，是空气艾达萌中最常见的一种，在大风天里经常能看到它们在风中起舞。它们通常有点焦虑、害羞，并对自己的外表感到不自在；泰洛噗很珍惜那些关心且支持它们的朋友，帮助它们成长并度过挣扎。当泰洛噗挣脱这些精神镣铐时，它们便会在天空中自由翱翔。

飞行艾达萌

由于它们与艾达萌训练师朋友之间的系绳联系，处于相位显现的、具有飞行速度的艾达萌必须在其回合结束时下降到地面；若它们不选择以常规移动下降到地面，它们会坠落，不过它们仍可如同正常的飞行生物一般进行“阻止坠落（Arrest the Fall）”。即便是通常具有幽影本质的灵体艾达萌，也会在其回合结束时贴着地面漂浮。若艾达萌训练师使用“融合相位（Melded Phase）”与艾达萌合体，则系绳不会限制它们，它们也不需要在其回合结束时下降到地面。

泰洛噗、夜翔翼（Nyctairis）与空翼龙（Airoptera）

空气

艾达萌

元素 空气

属性调整值 Str +0, Dex +3, Con +2, Int +1, Wis +4, Cha -1

艾达萌语言 苏苏兰语（Sussuran）

艾达萌生命值 4

艾达萌技能 特技、隐匿、生存

艾达萌豁免 反射

感官 弱光视野

抗力 空气、躯体、大地、植物、木质

弱点 寒冷、电击、金属、传送

速度 10尺，飞行速度25尺

近战 1 爪击（灵巧，娴熟；仅在进化后触及10尺）；伤害 1d4 挥砍（仅在进化后为1d6 挥砍）

近战 1 颌骨（娴熟，仅在进化后触及10尺）；伤害 1d4 穿刺（仅在进化后为1d6 穿刺）

进化状态 帮助泰洛噗克服其对自身身体以及吓到他人的焦虑。

元素能力 疾风速度 当你使用空气力量移动时，空气能量会让你更快。你在以空气力量移动时，你的各项速度获得+10尺状态加值。若你在力量DC上为专家，则提升为+20尺状态加值；若为大师，则为+30尺状态加值；若为传奇，则为+40尺状态加值。

形态能力 空气之翼 你的翅膀完全由空气构成，使你在飞行中比大多数其他生物具有显著更高的机动性。你进行“飞行机动（Maneuver in Flight）”与“阻止坠落（Arrest a Fall）”的特技检定获得+2环境加值。

进化能力 空气解放 空气的解放之力让你能够以不受限制的机动性移动。向上飞行对你而言不属于困难地形，并且即使你未与使用“融合相位（Melded Phase）”的艾达萌训练师合体，你也不必在回合结束时下降到地面。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.Lp5PLsGqdfRcekMM]{Tairopoof}

Tairopoofs, commonly known as "air bat eldamon," are the most common among air eldamon, and they can often be seen dancing among the winds on a blustery day. Usually a bit anxious, shy, and self-conscious about their appearance, tairopoofs appreciate caring and supportive friends who can help them grow and work through their struggles. When a tairopoof escapes these mental shackles, they soar freely across the sky.

Flying Eldamon

Due to their tether with their eldamon trainer friend, a phased in eldamon with a fly Speed must end its turn by descending to the ground; if they don't choose to move to the ground using their regular movement, they fall, though they can Arrest the Fall as normal for a flying creature. Even spirit eldamon, which are normally spectral in nature, end their turn floating along the ground. If the eldamon trainer is combined with the eldamon using Melded Phase, the tether doesn't restrict them, and they don't need to end their turn by descending to the ground.

Tairoproof, Nyctairis, and Airoptera

This increases to a +20-foot status bonus if you're an expert in power DC, a +30- foot status bonus if you're a master in power DC, and a +40-foot status bonus if you're legendary in power DC.

Form Ability Air Wings Your wings are formed entirely out of air, providing you significantly greater maneuverability in flight than most other creatures. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Acrobatics checks to Maneuver in Flight and Arrest a Fall.

Evolution Ability Air Unleashed The unleashed power of air allows you to move with unrestricted mobility. Flying upward isn't difficult terrain to you, and you don't have to end the turn by descending to the ground, even if you aren't combined with an eldamon trainer using Melded Phase.

Talir, Deity Of Dragons.name: 塔利尔，巨龙神祇 Talir, Deity Of Dragons
Talir, Deity Of Dragons.text:
第一者

阵营：N（LG、NG、CG、LN、N、CN、LE、NE、CE）

同胞：无

神殿：巨龙巢穴、远古怪兽战役遗址、塔利诺斯上的月之洞窟

崇拜者：巨龙、历史学家、梦想家、力量追求者、末日追寻者

圣兽：科莫多巨蜥

圣色：紫色与红色

领域：毁灭、放纵、威能、龙裔

牧师法术：1环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y6rAdMK6EFlV6U0t]{Breathe Fire}，6环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.5c692cCcTDXjSEzk]{Dragon Form}，9环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.2EIUqc8TCTQimggQ]{Summon Draconic Legion}

偏好武器：颌骨

神圣属性：力量或体质

神圣技能：运动

神授灌注：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm}

关键典范：将塔利尔从囚禁中解放出来；征服强者；吃掉你所杀之物并收取你应得之物

关键禁忌：靠诡计而非力量取胜；出于恐惧与无知迫害他者；不去饶恕战败者或赐予其死亡的解脱，反而将其囚禁

塔利尔作为双亲诸子中的长子，清除了在世间横行、肆虐并摧毁凡人与其文明的巨兽。即便在凡人代代相传的故事里，此时凡人也因畏惧其力量而转而反对塔利尔与其巨龙子嗣。随后，双亲为了迎合凡人的偏见，轻蔑并放逐了塔利尔——他们自己的孩子。自此塔利尔再也不同以往，转而寻求向双亲复仇，因其背叛并折磨了他们。每一个回合，双亲都诞下更多孩子来粉碎塔利尔的每一次谋划，最终将其囚禁于月亮塔利诺斯之中。

塔利尔的子嗣——诸多不同的巨龙传承——仍留在阿拉卡尔世界，作为一位在其他方面大多被遗忘或忽视的神祇所遗留的印记。然而即便如此，塔利尔仍能将其浩瀚力量的一丝涓流赐予真正虔信之人，通常是巨龙，或是研读古老记述并同情这位神祇的凡人研究者：他们渴求自身的巨大力量，或希望带来末日般的变革。就塔利尔而言，他们并不挑剔加入其神职者的对象。只要某人强大（心智、心灵、肉体或意志皆可，并无分别）且愿意承担塔利尔的事业，第一者便不顾其信条或道德而赐下他们的赋礼。

塔利尔在建立任何类似圣典或有组织宗教之前便已被逐出世界，因此对他们的崇拜往往以小型教派或邪教的形式零散出现，有些温和无害，有些则明显更为阴暗。最接近圣书之物的是《长子真理》，这是一部记述集，强调塔利尔与龙族才是受害者、凡人是挑衅者，而双亲则是背叛其子女的终极叛徒。甚至只有少数塔利尔的崇拜者曾见过这部文本，而见过的人往往也会对自己应当如何推进崇拜得出截然不同的结论。当一些追随者宣讲对那些外貌传统上被视作怪物者的宽容，而另一些则渴望吞噬敌人时，不难猜到他们并不总能意见一致。当两支不同的塔利尔崇拜者团体相遇时，他们极有可能相互战斗，以证明自身的力量与其信仰的力量。按根源，败者必须接受胜者对教义的看法——至少暂时如此。之后仍会有机会再战，以改变这一切。

不必多说，由于塔利尔的崇拜者终究想将他们的神祇从囚禁中解放出来，而据任何人所能判断，塔利尔很可能想吃掉双亲，以终结他们加诸塔利尔的折磨；因此，双亲的神职者以及平衡中大多数其他神祇都反对——并且常常试图取缔——对塔利尔的崇拜。哈瓦斯是唯一公开支持塔利尔教派者，尽管哈瓦斯的信徒往往更鼓励破坏性强、问题重重的教派，而非宽容仁善的教派。毕竟，助长一种吞噬一切的巨龙饥饿，能够帮助哈瓦斯实现其对匮乏与饥荒之理想世界的愿景。

巨龙的始祖

塔利尔不仅拥有惊人的力量，也拥有一种多面且多样、包罗万象的力量。通过吞噬威胁阿拉卡尔的巨兽并吸收其力量，塔利尔只会进一步增长其威能与多样性。这使他们得以孵化一窝又一窝、数十又数十打的龙蛋，每一窝都对应一种不同的巨龙传承。相应地，某一传承的巨龙常在艺术作品与故事中，将塔利尔描绘为与其传承共享独特的外貌与性情特征。

The First

Alignment: N (LG, NG, CG, LN, N, CN, LE, NE, CE)

Siblings: None

Temples: dragon lairs, sites of ancient kaiju battles, lunar caverns on Talinos

Worshippers: dragons, historians, dreamers, pursuers of strength, apocalypse seekers

Sacred Animal: komodo dragon

Sacred Colors: purple and red

Domains: destruction, indulgence, might, wyrmkin

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y6rAdMK6EFlV6U0t]{Breathe Fire}, 6th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.5c692cCcTDXjSEzk]{Dragon Form}, 9th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.2EIUqc8TCTQimggQ]{Summon Draconic Legion}

Favored Weapon: jaws

Divine Ability: Strength or Constitution

Divine Skill: Athletics

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm}

Key Edicts: release Talir from imprisonment, conquer the mighty, eat what you kill and collect what you earn

Key Anathema: win through trickery rather than strength, persecute others out of fear and ignorance, imprison the defeated rather than either spare them or grant them the release of death

Talir, firstborn of the Parents' children, rid the world of the massive beasts that rampaged and destroyed mortals and their civilizations. Even according to the tales mortals pass down, at this point, mortals turned against Talir and their draconic children out of fear of their power. At this point, the Parents spurned and banished Talir, their own child, in favor of the mortal's prejudice. Talir was never the same, seeking vengeance against the Parents for betraying and tormenting them. At each turn, the Parents birthed more children to crush each of Talir's plans, eventually imprisoning them within the moon Talinos.

Talir's children, the many different dragon heritages, remain on the world of Alacar, a legacy of a deity who is otherwise mostly forgotten or ignored. But yet, Talir can still grant a trickle of their enormous power to those of true faith, typically either dragons or else mortal researchers who read the ancient accounts and sympathized with the deity, desired great strength of their own, or wished to bring about apocalyptic change. For Talir's part, they aren't choosy about who joins their clergy. As long as someone is strong (of mind, heart, body, or will, it matters not) and willing to take up Talir's cause, the First grants their gifts without regard to ethos or morality.

Talir was banished from the world before establishing anything resembling a sacred text or organized religion, so their worship tends to pop up in small sects or cults, some benign and others significantly less so. The closest thing to a holy book is Truths of the Firstborn, a collection of accounts emphasizing Talir and dragonkind as the wronged party, mortals as the instigators, and the Parents as the ultimate traitors to their own child. Only a minority of worshippers of Talir have even come across this text, and those that do often come to starkly different conclusions about how they should proceed in their worship. When you have some followers preaching tolerance for those with appearances that seem classically monstrous and others seeking to devour their enemies, it's easy to guess that they won't always see eye to eye. When two separate groups of worshippers of Talir come together, it's extremely likely for them to fight each other to prove their own strength and that of their beliefs. By tradition, the losing group must accept the winning group's view of dogma, at least for now. There will be another chance later for a rematch to change that.

Needless to say, since Talir worshippers ultimately want to free their deity from imprisonment and Talir, as far as anyone can tell, probably wants to eat their Parents to end the torment they've heaped upon Talir, the clergy of the Parents and most other deities of the Balance oppose, and often attempt to outlaw, the worship of Talir. Havath stands alone as a proponent of Talir sects, though Havath's faithful tend to encourage destructive and problematic sects far more than tolerant and benevolent ones. After all, encouraging an all-consuming draconic hunger can assist in Havath's vision of an ideal world of scarcity and famine.

Progenitor of Dragons

Talir possesses not only amazing power but also a multifarious and diverse power that contains multitudes. By devouring the enormous beasts that threatened Alacar and absorbing their strength, Talir only increased their might and diversity further. This allowed them to hatch dozens and dozens of clutches of eggs, each with a different dragon heritage. For their part, dragons of a given heritage often depict Talir as sharing their heritage's own distinctive physical and temperamental features in artwork and stories.

Indigo Isles Familiars.text:
The interplay of elements has a strong influence on the world of Alacar. Eldamon are among the most obvious manifestations of this primal power, but it is also reflected in which of Alacar’s creatures take most readily to the magic that turns a mundane creature into a familiar. While all of these familiars are uncommon, characters from the Indigo Isles have access to them.

Astroloptera

Astrolopteras are bat-like creatures with large ears, blue or purple fur, and starry wings. Favored by occult spellcasters, they sense and read subtle cosmic vibrations to divine predictions of the future. They are also known for engaging in cryptic musings. After important events occur, they often point back to their past musings and confidently assert that they had accurately predicted what would transpire. How much any individual astrolopteras actually predicts the future is hard to discern.

Cosmic Insight 2 Frequency Once per day; Effect An astroloptera can peer into the near future, granting their master a vision of a possible future. Select one action that the astroloptera’s master could reasonably perform in the next minute that requires rolling a 1d20, and roll that d20 in advance. The astroloptera’s master automatically receives that result the first time they take that action within the next minute; if they do not wish to receive that result, they can instead spend 1 action with the manipulate trait to perform a quick series of gestures to ward off ill fortune; if they do so, they instead roll the d20 associated with the check normally.

Echolocation An astroloptera has echolocation as a precise sense with a range of 20 feet.


Embersap Ooze

According to legend, the first embersap oozes were created long ago by a grove of wildfire leshys, who sought to breathe a spark of life into tree sap in the aftermath of a forest fire. Embersap oozes seek companionship to ensure that their body temperatures don’t get too cold and to defend against potential predators, and often travel together in embersap ooze swarms. However, some embersap oozes form a special bond with a single creature who imbues them with an additional spark of intelligence, becoming familiars. This spark also magically endows them with vision and hearing, senses which the familiars find strange and intriguing, and which inspire their natural desire to explore. When the temperature drops, however, they want little to do with the outside world. They hide away snugly in scarves, coats, and bedrolls, making them an excellent companion for cold nights. To assist in communication with people who are accustomed to looking at faces, emberspark ooze familiars often display pieces of charcoal or other fire-resistant objects on the front of their bodies to approximate eyes and a mouth. They manipulate these objects to change their apparent expressions, and may occasionally throw them at people to get their attention or express extreme annoyance.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actors.Actor.0GjLWbFZ7DajZU7Q]{EMBERSAP OOZE}

Cauterize 2 Frequency once per hour; Effect The embersap ooze flows over an open wound and draws all of its heat onto its surface, giving an immediate flat check to end the bleed with the lower DC for particularly effective assistance.

Enflame Weapon 2 Frequency once per day; Effect The embersap ooze can reshape its body to coat a melee weapon, and then set its outer surface ablaze. For 1 minute or until the embersap ooze resumes its original form, the weapon deals 1 additional fire damage per weapon damage die.

Frozen Sap Exposure to cold dulls an embersap ooze’s flames and solidifies the sap that forms its body. It gains weakness to cold equal to your level. You can spend an additional familiar ability, either permanently or on a per-day basis, to reinforce the embersap ooze’s body, removing this weakness.

Ooze Defense Your embersap ooze familiar is easy to hit, but it lacks weak points. It is immune to critical hits and precision damage, but its AC is only 10 + your level (instead of an AC equal to yours).


Mechanical Parrot

Mechanical Parrots are graceful constructs made from a fusion of intricately carved metal parts and gems infused with air magic. G’mayun creators typically use several colors of metal and gems to give color to their mechanical parrot’s plumage. The precursors to mechanical parrot familiars were parrot-shaped constructs that could only record sound, and while the familiars retain that ability, they are capable of saying much, much more. In fact, mechanical parrots are inveterate chatterboxes with keen memories, so those who wish to keep their secrets safe had best not utter them in earshot of these familiars.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actors.Actor.HVdGAqsS3y866QoE]{MECHANICAL PARROT}

construct
