Granted Abilities @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.ZHSzNt3NxkXbj1mI]{Flier}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.cT5octWchU4gjrhP]{Manual Dexterity}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.jevzf9JbJJibpqaI]{Skilled} (Deception), @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.zyMRLQnFCQVpltiR]{Speech}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.9PsptrEoCC4QdM23]{Valet}

Constructed A mechanical parrot is immune to bleed, death effects, disease, doomed, drained, fatigued, healing, necromancy, nonlethal attacks, paralyzed, poison, sickened, and unconscious. It can recover Hit Points only via the Repair action and other methods that fix items, not from any form of Hit Point healing. It’s destroyed at 0 HP.

Record Once per day, your mechanical parrot can listen carefully to and memorizes a snippet up to 1 minute worth of sound down to the slightest detail. The parrot can then reproduce the sound at any time on command. When repeating a memorized snippet, the parrot gains a +4 circumstance bonus on Deception checks to sound like the original source. A mechanical parrot can only remember one snippet at a time in this way; once it memorizes a new snippet, the previous one is lost.


Seafoam Spirit

Seafoam spirits are manifestations of the world’s life force embodied in water (so like "leshy spirits," they aren’t literal spirits). Their boundless curiosity drives them to explore the world beyond the ocean’s grasp. While their natural form is an invisible force animating about a gallon of water, they can effortlessly and fluidly transform their shape, taking on the form of ocean-dwelling animals. When in water, these animals may look like a typical member of their species. On land, they can be easily identified by the wave of seawater that engulfs their bodies and surrounds them everywhere they go.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actors.Actor.lQ3jgdOYAJsTYfUX]{SEAFOAM SPIRIT}

uncommon

water

Required Number of Abilities 3

Granted Abilities @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.A0C86V3MUECpX5jd]{Amphibious}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.lpyJAl5B4j2DC7mc]{Gills}

Change Shape 1 (concentrate, primal, polymorph, transmutation) The seafoam changes its shape from its default form as an amorphous blob of water to the form of a Tiny aquatic animal that lives in saltwater. It can use this action again to return to its natural shape or adopt a new shape. Using Change Shape counts as creating a disguise for the Impersonate use of Deception. The seafoam spirit’s transformation automatically defeats Perception DCs to determine whether it is an aquatic animal, and it gains a +4 status bonus to its Deception DC to prevent others from seeing through its disguise. The seafoam spirit doesn’t gain any special abilities of the new shape, only its physical form. As a part of this transformation, the seafoam spirit can swap out any familiar abilities specifically based on its physical form to better fit its new form (though it can’t swap out amphibious or gills, since it always changes to an aquatic creature, which means only a seafoam spirit with more than the minimum number of familiar abilities will have enough abilities to switch them out). For example, a seafoam spirit might have darkvision to represent its form as a deep sea anglerfish with ability to see in the dark that it swaps out for burrower when it transforms into an oyster.

Evaporation Under normal conditions, seafoam spirits can extract enough water from the air, soil, puddles, or other sources to replenish what they lose. When in particularly arid environments such as a desert, seafoam spirits experience a slow evaporation of their bodies. They must consume at least 1 gallon of water every 2 hours in such environments or become slowed 1 until they replenish their lost water. If a seafoam spirit goes at least 24 hours in an arid environment without water, they fall into a state of torpor and do not awaken until rehydrated. Seafoam spirits have weakness 5 to effects based on dehydration.

Waveglider A seafoam spirit is always surrounded by a wave of seawater that moves along with it, allowing it to Swim wherever it goes. This water provides a buffer between the spirit and airborne toxins, granting it a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against inhaled effects.


Shale Hatchling

Adventurers and treasure hunters alike prize the crystals that shale spitters naturally produce, but shale spitters are notoriously territorial. Those who take a hatchling as a familiar can forge a lasting bond with the creature. Shale hatchling familiars magically retain their Tiny size. As their master gains power, their appetites grow, and they produce ever greater amounts of precious crystal. A well-fed shale hatchling emits a low rumbling sound reminiscent of a cat’s purr. When hungry, afraid, or otherwise distressed, shale hatchings typically curl up into a ball.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actors.Actor.IfG1z3LJ2WqSVh1S]{SHALE HATCHLING}

uncommon

animal

earth

Required Number of Abilities 4

Granted Abilities @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.uy15sDBuYNK48N3v]{Burrower}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.0Xrkk46IM43iI1Fv]{Darkvision}

Bloodless Shale hatchlings lack blood, so they are immune to bleed damage.

Crystal Generation If your shale hatching familiar is conscious and healthy, you can use Nature to Earn Income, by spending your time carefully feeding your familiar and harvesting its precious crystalline waste.

Curl Up 1 The shale hatchling recoils into its carapace, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC. It retains this bonus to AC and is slowed 1 until it uncurls by spending an action (this action has the move trait).

Rock Stride Shale hatchlings ignore difficult terrain in caves and rocky mountains.


Toogtoog

Toogtoogs are cunning, chubby, tiny, froglike creatures that are popular on the Indigo Isles as pets, both because of their cute features and because of the continuous supply of ink they produce from the inkwells on the top of their heads. On the other hand, chefs sometimes consider them pests when they gather in swarms, also called "spills" and devour their tasty ingredients. Toogtoog familiars can infuse this ink with magical properties, and even transform themselves into ink to become a living tattoo on their master’s body.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actors.Actor.2GDZWgrKZ8QuPelv]{TOOGTOOG}

uncommon

animal

Required Number of Abilities 2

Granted Abilities @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.dWTfO5WbLkD5mw2H]{Climber}

Inkwell A typical healthy toogtoog produces enough ink to fill a standard ink vial every day. Toogtoog familiars can also choose to produce a different type of ink that is well-suited for tattoos. This faintly magical ink is nontoxic and resistant to fading. The toogtoog’s master can recolor this ink at will, allowing them to alter the color of tattoos or even make them invisible if desired. Despite being a minion, your toogtoog familiar gains 1 reaction at the start of its turns, which it can use only to Aid you on a Crafting check to create a tattoo or other ink-based artwork (it still has to prepare to help you as normal for the Aid reaction). It automatically succeeds at its check to Aid you with those skills or automatically critically succeeds if you’re a master of the skill in question.

Toogtoog Tattoo A stylized tattoo of a toogtoog appears on your body. The toogtoog can spend a single action, which has the magical, move, and transmutation traits, to meld into or exit the tattoo. It must be adjacent to you to meld into your tattoo, and it exits your tattoo in an open space adjacent to you. Any of the familiar’s companion items remain on it, but it can’t carry any other items with it. While melded in the tattoo, the only action it can use is the action to exit the tattoo. If your toogtoog familiar dies, this tattoo loses its magic and becomes a mundane tattoo. You can alter the color of the tattoo’s ink as normal for tattoos produced by toogtoog ink.


Wildfire Leshy

Whether due to exposure to flames or by the nature of the materials from which it was created, wildfire leshy familiars carry a spark of fire with them everywhere they go. Druids are typically the only ones who can earn the trust of these tiny manifestations of natural renewal, though other determined and patient spellcasters have occasionally succeeded in their efforts to forge familiar bonds with them.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actors.Actor.AimSIbDqUdH4ve2d]{WILDFIRE LESHY}

uncommon

leshy

plant

Required Number of Abilities 4 (must be a leshy familiar)

Granted Abilities @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.cT5octWchU4gjrhP]{Manual Dexterity}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.57b8u8s3fV0UCrgJ]{Plant Form}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.zyMRLQnFCQVpltiR]{Speech}, @Compendium[pf2e.familiar-abilities.zKTL9y9et0oTHEYS]{Greater Resistance} (fire)

Self-Immolate 2 (evocation, fire, primal) Frequency Once per hour Effect Your wildfire leshy familiar can flare up its own burning embers, creating a thick plume of opaque smoke where it stands in a 5-foot burst. The wildfire leshy loses 4 Hit Points. All creatures within the affected area are concealed, and all other creatures are concealed to them. The smoke persists for 1 minute or until it is dispersed by a strong wind.

Smoke Vision Smoke doesn’t impair a wildfire leshy’s vision; it ignores concealment from smoke.

Water Weakness Your wildfire leshy familiar has weakness to water equal to your level. In addition to damaging effects with the water trait, as normal, the weakness applies when your wildfire leshy is exposed to a nondamaging water effect, such as when it’s doused with water (either through a spell or some other effect, such as pouring a bucket of water over it or being caught in the rain). You can spend an additional familiar ability, either permanently or on a per-day basis, to reinforce your wildfire leshy and waterproof it, removing this weakness.

Archetypes of the Isles.name: 群岛的变体 Archetypes of the Isles
Archetypes of the Isles.text:
The Indigo Isles are home to countless techniques rarely seen on the continents or even other nearby islands. These include special pirate tricks that enhance the existing pirate archetype and a pair of brand new archetypes unique to the Isles!

Indigo Isles Pirate Feats

In the Indigo Isles, pirates are both common and complicated. While the islands certainly have their share of traditional pirates—ruthless, bloodthirsty raiders who have no qualms with the blatant theft and murder of any ship they can catch—even more common are performative adventuring pirates who act as rakish romantic heroes. Though in many regions bloodthirsty pirates scoff at these adventuring pirates, considering them little more than children playing dress-up as pirates, it's hard to dismiss adventuring pirates in the Indigo Isles because of the legacy of Poppy, the Gilded Pirate.

Poppy, the region's greatest hero, was the one who started the regional trend of cosmetic piracy, with all the trappings of pirates without actually engaging in malicious skullduggery. Indeed, the consensus for heroic and adventurous pirates means that the most wicked and villainous among the pirates of the Indigo Isles are often defeated and captured, or left marooned on isolated Moonshadow Island. Nonetheless, the pirates of the region run the gamut, and often it's impossible to judge a particular pirate crew's intentions without prior experience. This means that from the perspective of merchants and other travelers, it's safer to just avoid all the pirates, just in case.

The Redfeathers, the royal family of Rumplank and descendants of Poppy's legacy, help keep the status quo of relatively benevolent piracy via a series of policies intended to maintain it. For instance, merchants who pay their tariffs and taxes receive a certification and a flag they can fly, and nearly all pirates follow a rule that disallows attacking such merchants. Pirates who are registered and can prove they've kept to these terms receive a payoff from the government, and they're free to go after unregistered pirates, smugglers, and other ne'er-do-wells.

Due to this situation, various pirates are famous, respected, and even considered trendy in the Indigo Isles. This means many children grow up dreaming of becoming pirates rather than being forced into piracy only in the worst of circumstances, and in turn, it continues to reinforce the cycle of heroic piracy. In the modern era, many such would-be pirates roam the streets of Rumplank, Seaview, and other settlements, and they run the gamut among social classes and backgrounds.

Acceptable Targets

The more adventurous and heroic pirates of the Indigo Isles still engage in piracy and raids. Otherwise, how could they call themselves pirates? However, they don't ply their trade against innocents or the defenseless. Instead, they battle monsters, villains, and evil pirates alike in glorious battle, plundering their foes' ill-gotten gains.

Additional Pirate Feats

The following feats are available to characters with the pirate archetype, in addition to the feats found in Pathfinder Advanced Player’s Guide. They are all listed as uncommon, and they might not be appropriate for stereotypical pirates in other regions, but pirates from the Indigo Isles have access to all of them.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.Iakf10foOKasvQnB]{Booty Finder}FEAT 4

uncommon

archetype

Prerequisites Pirate Dedication

You have an innate sense for treasure and booty, and your larcenous nature and cupidity mean that you agonize over any such loot you might have missed through lack of searching. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to find hidden treasure and when using Recall Knowledge to determine the value of treasure. Even if you aren’t Searching, the GM rolls you a secret check to find hidden treasure that normally requires you to be Searching. You still need to meet any other requirements to find the treasure.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.Jid6Y1LW8yD8GEfl]{The Old Rumplank Snatch & Grab}FEAT 6

uncommon

archetype

Prerequisites Pirate Dedication

You rush past a foe and attack them, distracting them and cutting their purse or otherwise forcefully taking their valuables. You Stride. At any point during your Stride, you can make a melee Strike against a single creature. If the Strike hits, you can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.RDXXE7wMrSPCLv5k]{Steal} from the target, even if the target is in combat. You still must meet any other requirements for the Steal action (they must be in reach, you need a free hand, and you can only Steal certain types of items, as specified in the action’s description).

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.gXR24Tw1Nfd94lBB]{The Dead Tell No Tales}FEAT 7

uncommon

archetype

skill

Prerequisites Pirate Dedication

You remind others that the dead keep secrets best of all, scaring them into silence. Due to your fearsome reputation threatening others into silence (and explicit activities you take to do so such as using the @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.tHCqgwjtQtzNqVvd]{Coerce} action), it becomes much more difficult to gain information you’re trying to suppress. Checks to @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.DCb62iCBrJXy0Ik6]{Request} the information, @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.plBGdZhqq5JBl1D8]{Gather Information}, or otherwise get someone to divulge the information in question when you’re actively attempting to suppress it, must exceed your Intimidation DC, in addition to all other relevant DCs. If they succeed at the other DC but not against your Intimidation DC, they are able to determine that people know the information but are too scared to reveal what it is.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.t1i2V1IJZoS9DoHT]{Yo Ho Ho and a Bottle of Rum!}FEAT 10

uncommon

archetype

Prerequisites Pirate Dedication

Frequency once per hour

You withdraw and swig a drink of alcohol in order to overcome a difficult situation and give yourself a dose of liquid courage. Interact to draw a drink of alcohol and then Interact again to drink it; this can’t be a special alchemical alcohol that has additional effects beyond the base effects of alcohol. You gain 3 temporary Hit Points per level for 2 rounds and attempt a counteract check against a mental effect currently affecting you, using your Will save modifier as the counteract modifier and half your level rounded up as the counteract level. If the counteract check doesn’t remove the mental effect, you can’t try to remove it again with Yo Ho Ho and a Bottle of Rum! You don’t have to attempt a saving throw against the alcohol, and you don’t gain any other special benefits from other abilities that require you to drink alcohol, or that occur when you drink alcohol.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.VqDzjsk1LigkEnE5]{X Marks the Spot}FEAT 4

uncommon

archetype

Prerequisites Pirate Dedication

You make two quick attacks against your foe, forming an X and marking a vulnerability for your allies to exploit. Strike twice against the same foe. If both Strikes hit, the foe is @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg] until your next turn, and each of your allies gain a +1 circumstance bonus on their next attack rolls against that foe before your next turn. If both Strikes are critical hits, the circumstance bonus is +2 instead of +1.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.t1QjrZlGIORcyli6]{Poppy's Peerless Pierce}FEAT 12

uncommon

archetype

Prerequisites Pirate Dedication

Using a piratical technique passed down by Poppy von Barnacle and mastered by few others since her heyday, you effortlessly pierce a foe’s defenses and attack a foe behind them as well. Make a melee Strike with a piercing weapon against a foe within reach. This Strike ignores an amount of the foe’s Hardness or resistance equal to your level. If your attack hits and damages the target, you can compare the result of your attack roll to the AC of a foe adjacent to the first target and in a straight line behind them. Use the same damage roll against the second foe. You can attack the second foe even if they would normally be 5 feet beyond the edge of your reach. Poppy’s Peerless Pierce counts as two attacks for the purpose of calculating your multiple attack penalty.

Rumplank Reveler (Archetype)

Nobody throws a party like Rumplank, and no one knows that better than you! Many have taken part in the hospitality of the City of Colors, but when you immersed yourself in the party, you found your true calling. You became one with the revelry and emerged with a zealous fervor for festivities and a talent for leading others down the same path. As a Rumplank reveler, you might not always stay in the city that has forever become a part of your soul, but wherever you go, you bring the party along with you!

Additional Feats: 4th @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.Btu0tA6SEBS6K1hE]{Never Tire}; 7th @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.wqhxZwB1TR8fvpHP]{Party Crasher}; 10th @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.E7eceezD3NDmBVBb]{Back To Back}

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.alyrBcRRQu9UbTFH]{Rumplank Reveler Dedication}FEAT 2

uncommon

archetype

dedication

Prerequisites trained in Performance; trained in Alcohol Lore or Cooking Lore

Access You have the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-backgrounds.Item.slh3lyeHxQoNtBUi]{Rumplank Reveler} background or have participated heavily in Rumplank festivities.

You are a merry-making carouser capable of rallying any crowd into a contagious celebration of cheerfulness and gaiety. You become trained in Alcohol Lore or Cooking Lore if you aren't trained in both. If you were already trained in Alcohol Lore or Cooking, you become an expert in each of those Lore skills in which you were trained instead. You become an expert in Performance, and you gain the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} action.

Special You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Rumplank reveler archetype.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.snLlEc35vrpTZIT0]{Keep This Party Going}FEAT 4

archetype

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

Requirements You are benefiting from @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel}

You know how to keep a party going by passing out extra food, pouring your best drinks, or simply kicking things into high gear with everyone's favorite song. Attempt a check in the skill chosen for your Revel. The DC is usually a standard-difficulty DC of a level equal to the highest-level character benefiting from your Revel, but the GM can assign a different DC based on the circumstances.

Critical Success The Revel lasts until the end of your turn after next. Until the beginning of your next turn, the status bonus to saving throws increases to +2.

Success The Revel lasts until the end of your next turn, or its current duration, whichever is longer.

Critical Failure The Revel ends immediately.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.F1sRVd9Kd5PP9zUB]{Party Planner}FEAT 6

archetype

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

By planning parties in advance, you can prepare to host a celebration at a moment's notice. When you roll initiative, you can @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} as a free action. If you're legendary in Alcohol Lore, Cooking Lore, or Performance you can use a reaction to Revel when you critically succeed at an attack roll with an improvised weapon associated with your skill (such as a tankard, frying pan, or guitar).

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.qreAGIAKpvZJxEpG]{Battle Canapes}FEAT 7

archetype

skill

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication, master in Alcohol Lore or Cooking Lore

You can pass around prepared drinks and payayos to give your guests a quick burst of energy. You consume the canapes yourself or administer them to an ally benefiting from @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel}, either adjacent to you or connected to you by a chain of any number of contiguous Reveling allies who pass the canapes along. Attempt a @Check[type:Alcohol Lore|dc:30] or @Check[type:Cooking Lore|dc:30] check. If you have legendary proficiency in one of those skills, you can instead attempt a @Check[type:Alcohol Lore|dc:40] or @Check[type:Cooking Lore|dc:40] check to increase the temporary Hit Points gained on a successful check to 15 (25 on a critical success).

Critical Success The target gains 10 temporary Hit Points. They lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute or at the end of the encounter, whichever comes first.

Success The target gains 5 temporary Hit Points. They lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute or at the end of the encounter, whichever comes first.

Failure The canapes have no effect.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.ghYrvkvLXfCMgBeu]{Boogie Down}FEAT 8

archetype

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication, master in Performance

Your performance is so catchy that those around you can't help but want to follow along with your choreography. You Stride in one direction. If your Stride is successful, all creatures affected by @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} get +10-foot status bonus to their Speed for 1 round as long as they move in the same direction that you moved.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.oHv2imP0M4UVv3Wb]{Party Foul}FEAT 10

archetype

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

Trigger A creature benefiting from @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} rolls a critical failure, or rolls a critical success using an action that targets a creature also benefiting from the same Revel.

You call out when someone in your party makes a mistake, giving them an opportunity to correct themselves. The target rerolls the check and is immune to Party Foul for 24 hours. If the triggering action was a critical failure, this reaction has the fortune trait. If the triggering action was a critical success, this reaction has the misfortune trait, and you end the benefit of Revel for the triggering creature; if the triggering creature was a foe, they become temporarily immune to your Revel for 24 hours.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.n3VGtnTPxacFYjYd]{Crowd Favorite}FEAT 12

archetype

Auditory

Concentrate

Emotion

Linguistic

Mental

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication, master in Alcohol Lore, Cooking Lore, or Performance

You know exactly what song to play or food to serve to make everyone at the party feel connected, at ease, and welcome. At the end of each of your turns during a @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel}, attempt a skill check associated with the skill you chose to Revel. The DC is usually a standard-difficulty DC of a level equal to the highest-level character benefiting from your Revel, but the GM can assign a different DC based on the circumstances.

Critical Success Reduce the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened} value of all creatures affected by your Revel by 2.

Success Reduce the frightened value of all creatures affected by your Revel by 1.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.nq3lte4pAncp4hY4]{Everyone! Sashay!}FEAT 15

archetype

Auditory

Concentrate

Emotion

Linguistic

Mental

Skill

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

You order everyone dancing along with your music to move at once in the same direction. You Stride in one direction. At the same time, all willing allies affected by @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} use a reaction to Stride in the same direction. Creatures stop moving if this movement would put them in immediate danger. Foes affected by Revel must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:resolve(@actor.system.skills.prf.dc)] saving throw against your Performance DC, with the following effects.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

Success The foe is unaffected.

Failure The foe is forced to Stride, but it doesn't need to use its reaction to do so.

Critical Failure The foe must use its reaction to Stride. If it doesn't have a reaction to Stride, you can choose whether to force it to Stride anyway or to have it remain in place.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.XRnKyF1Zuf944Tg0]{Kick it up a Notch}FEAT 15

archetype

skill

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

Requirements You are holding non-magical food or drink and an elixir, ingested poison, or potion, or you are wearing them and have free hands.

You can season your canapes with a surprise kick. If your next action is to use @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.qreAGIAKpvZJxEpG]{Battle Canapes}, you can add an elixir, ingested poison, or potion to the ingested food or drink, applying its effects to the ally you chose in addition to the temporary Hit Points from Battle Canapes. Applying harmful or unwanted effects to your allies' food or drink is an evil action.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.K5JpDb3yCLNa1Bf5]{Rowdy Reveler}FEAT 16

archetype

fortune

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

You can incite a crowd into frenzied fervor, often making things more dangerous for everyone. You and each ally benefiting from @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} can choose to roll two dice when attempting a saving throw or skill check, once per round each. If they do, they choose one result to use for the check and use the other result for a @Check[type:flat|dc:11] with the following results.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} until the end of their next turn.

Critical Failure The creature is clumsy 2 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} until the end of their next turn or for the duration of the Revel, whichever is longer.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.5OOh3xV7AYsOwXt9]{Life of the Party}FEAT 18

archetype

Prerequisites Rumplank Reveler Dedication

Frequency once every 10 minutes

You refuse to even let death get in the way of your guests enjoyment and ruin your party. You administer a special drink or offer a rallying toast to an adjacent creature who has died while benefiting from @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.M2DHoTWpUrQKGlzH]{Revel} no more than 2 rounds ago. When you do, that creature is immediately returned to life with 1 Hit Point and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded 1}.


Toxicant Sentinel (Archetype)

On Bluebell Isle, a group of extremely steadfast kragrak eldamon trainers known as the Dedication used their powers since ancient times to save the island from the expansion of a region of deadly purple toxins called the Violet Woodlands.

According to kragrak legends, long ago, these amaranthine woodlands choked out more or less the entire Bluebell Isle, with the exception of a tiny sanctuary in the Mist, where creatures that weren't resistant to violet poison could survive, at least temporarily, and seek refuge from the devouring poison. For a time, this was enough.

When the violet poison began infesting even into the Mist, the desperate denizens emerged from their sanctuary and burned down much of the forest, heedless of the deadly fumes that their deforestation caused. In an attempt to prevent the cycle from continuing until everything on the island died, supposedly, the kragraks of the time used druidic magic to commune with the woodlands and broker a sort of detente between poisonous flora and the surviving ancestries of the island. The purple wildlife would remain restricted to the Violet Woodlands, leaving the rest of the island to the other creatures and ancestries in exchange for a promise to let them flourish within those boundaries without putting them to the torch. And so Bluebell Isle became populated once again.

Even to this day, the kragraks of the Dedication remain sworn adjudicators of this ancient promise, or so their founding story states. But were these wardens to fail at their duty or fall in battle, the woodlands would likely spread until they devoured the entire island.

To prevent that apocalyptic fate, a second order arose. Calling themselves the toxicant sentinels, these fearless protectors were formed from volunteers of every ancestry from across the Indigo Isles. The toxicant sentinels banded together for the explicit purpose of protecting the kragrak eldamon trainers of the Dedication as bodyguards and companions, using unique techniques of poison elemental magic to enhance their martial prowess and survive the inhospitable Violet Woodlands.

While this means many toxicant sentinels spend most of their time in the Violet Woodlands guarding the members of the Dedication who can never leave those confines, there are also those who travel elsewhere to promote the old oaths far and wide. After all, where the Dedication can't see, the toxicant sentinels must still keep watch, and where the Dedication can't go, the toxicant sentinels must scout ahead. Little by little, the two groups learned how to work together to achieve greater goals on Bluebell Isle and across the Indigo Isles, though the toxicant sentinels always worked in the shadows of the more famous and eye- catching kragrak eldamon trainers.

You've passed your initiation, and so you are now a toxicant sentinel. As such, you've learned how to harness some of the poison elemental magic secrets kept by the eldamon trainers of the Dedication for countless generations, protecting your own body from the Violet Woodlands' toxins while allowing you to exude flesh- eating poison to weaken your foes and proactively protect your allies.

You've sworn a solemn oath to protect the Dedication, but that doesn't mean that your entire life is limited to the confines of the Violet Woodlands. Missions often take you beyond those boundaries, even though the Dedication members themselves can rarely leave, allowing you to act on behalf of both organizations from afar when needed.

Additional Feats: 4th @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.0lieFKdwgNVXep7u]{Poison Resistance}

Describing Your Toxin

As a toxicant sentinel, all of your abilities center around a toxin growing within you, a strain of the violet poison from the Violet Woodlands that's safe to you and protects you from the woodlands' malaise. Whenever you use a toxicant sentinel ability, be sure to describe the purple toxin welling up inside you as you apply their effects. The visual cues not only make your abilities feel more evocative, they're also an important clue to your allies and enemies that something is unusual about you. The bright purple color might even throw off foes that are animals or have otherwise learned to avoid certain colors as poisonous.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.TSvE6Q89LRPLCG9l]FEAT 2

uncommon

archetype

dedication

Access You are a member of the toxicant sentinels who guard the wardens of the Violet Woodlands.

You've been initiated into the toxicant sentinels. You can safely live in the Violet Woodlands, despite the poison that suffuses the environment there. This doesn't provide absolute protection from poisonous hazards and monsters in the Violet Woodlands, only from the ambient environmental poison. Additionally, you gain the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.4ZDb3iddvdjckn00]{Exude Toxin} action.

Special You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Toxicant Sentinel Archetype.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.dAzrSJvH0J5kLg1g]FEAT 4

archetype

skill

Prerequisites Toxicant Sentinel Dedication, expert in Nature

Your knowledge of toxins is based on the principles of the natural world, but you can apply those same ideas to understand all sorts of poisons, even synthetic poisons. You can use Nature instead of Crafting to identify any type of poison, including alchemical poisons. You can also use Nature to Craft poisons.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.NUHcJWnab2IrsCZx]FEAT 4

archetype

Prerequisites Toxicant Sentinel Dedication

You can make your toxin linger in your foe's system. When you use @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.4ZDb3iddvdjckn00]{Exude Toxin}, you can choose to deal persistent poison or acid damage (whichever would deal more damage) instead of normal additional damage.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.tEzIlQX9BhlILa5a]FEAT 6

archetype

Prerequisites Toxicant Sentinel Dedication

Your toxin sickens your foe whenever it spreads fully through their system. On a critical hit with a Strike that applies the damage from @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.4ZDb3iddvdjckn00]{Exude Toxin}, if the target takes acid or poison damage, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}. If you have the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.7Jtrhy0d1lgKZvUc]{Sweat Toxin} feat, the target is also sickened 1 on a critical failure if they take acid or poison damage. If you deal persistent damage to the foe with @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.NUHcJWnab2IrsCZx]{Persistent Toxin}, the foe can't reduce their sickened value below 1 while the persistent damage lasts.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.7Jtrhy0d1lgKZvUc]FEAT 8

archetype

Prerequisites Toxicant Sentinel Dedication

Trigger An adjacent foe hits you with a melee weapon attack or a foe hits you with a melee unarmed attack

You sweat toxin from your skin, exposing the foe who hit you to your toxin. You deal your @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.4ZDb3iddvdjckn00]{Exude Toxin} damage to the triggering foe, who attempts a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:resolve(@actor.system.attributes.classDC.value)|basic:true] save against your class DC. If you have the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.NUHcJWnab2IrsCZx]{Persistent Toxin} feat, you can use it to deal persistent damage instead when you Sweat Toxin.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.glMAYrhNPIxV0XFV]FEAT 10

archetype

Prerequisites Persistent Toxin

It's easy for you to strike your foe down while they're ravaged by your toxin. Any creature suffering from persistent acid damage or persistent poison damage from your toxin is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg] to you as long as they're suffering from the persistent damage. If they're immune to both acid and poison damage, they aren't off-guard to you.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.vuVivp8UKxeuYcK7]FEAT 12

archetype

Prerequisites Sweat Toxin

You've learned how to use your poisonous sweat to protect your allies, as well as to protect yourself from ranged attacks. You can use your @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.7Jtrhy0d1lgKZvUc]{Sweat Toxin} reaction if a foe within 15 feet of you hits you or an ally within 15 feet of you with an attack (whether or not it's a melee attack), in addition to its normal trigger.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.P0OJiiY2wH0Gfn9R]FEAT 16

archetype

Prerequisites Toxicant Sentinel Dedication

Your toxin leaps from victim to victim. On a critical hit when you @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-actions.Item.4ZDb3iddvdjckn00]{Exude Toxin} or a foe's critical failure against @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.7Jtrhy0d1lgKZvUc]{Sweat Toxin}, you expose a secondary foe adjacent to the original foe, with a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:resolve(@actor.system.attributes.classDC.value)|basic:true] save against your class DC. If a secondary foe continues to critically fail their saving throw, you can continue to expose additional secondary foes, though you can't expose the same foe to your toxin more than once in total for each use of Exude Toxin or Sweat Toxin.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.Sn8BGrmyqJfEkcjD]FEAT 18

archetype

Prerequisites Sweat Toxin

You've perfected your ability to sweat toxin, and now it comes to you reflexively. At the start of each of your turns when you regain your actions, you gain an additional reaction that can be used only to @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-feats.Item.7Jtrhy0d1lgKZvUc]{Sweat Toxin}.

The Legend of Old Woody.name: 老伍迪传奇 The Legend of Old Woody
The Legend of Old Woody.text:
In addition to the items found elsewhere in the world, the Indigo Isles are home to countless magical and alchemical brews, including the legendary Old Woody.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-equipment.qXglq7r16rnG6Gjc]{Old Woody} ITEM 6

rare

consumable

morph

primal

transmutation

Usage held in 1 hand; Bulk L

Activate 1 Interact

Old Woody is a legendary brew, a strange magical alcohol that produces randomized effects. Its price and taste vary wildly based on the year of the brew, which can not only change the taste, but also cause it to have different effects than the ones listed below, or make some of the effects more likely than others. Some brews of Old Woody even have a different item level. A single drink from a typical brew of Old Woody sells for around 50 gp, if you can find one. After drinking Old Woody, roll a [[/r 1d100]]{d100}, and apply the listed effect. If you didn’t willingly drink the Old Woody, you can attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20|traits:consumable,morph,primal,transmutation] save to negate the effect. After drinking Old Woody, you are temporarily immune to the effects of further drinks of Old Woody for 24 hours.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.roll-tables.HNjiGT7lPSMZuZpW]{Old Woody}

D100 Effect Name

Mechanical Effect

01-03 Life Of The Party

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you become charismatic but vapid and unintelligent. You gain a +2 item bonus to Charisma-based skill checks, and a –2 item penalty to Intelligence-based skill checks. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.ZF5cvbrehT0UgJaW]{Effect: Life Of The Party}

04-06 That’s Deep, Man

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you stagger slowly as you contemplate the secrets of the universe. You gain a +2 item bonus to Will saves, Perception, and Wisdom-based skill checks, and a –2 item penalty to AC, Reflex saves, and Dexterity-based skill checks. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.HGRP3TWuJnsyoRyp]{Effect: That's Deep, Man}

07-09 You Gotta Problem?

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, your muscles bulge but you suffer from a quick temper and poor impulse control. You gain a +2 item bonus to Strength-based attack rolls, Strength-based skill checks, and Strength-based damage rolls, and a –2 item penalty to Will saves, Perception, and Wisdom-based skill checks. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.qtmGI3YNIT4C9glt]{Effect: You Gotta Problem?}

10-12 Hard Headed

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you become resilient at the expense of your social skills. You gain 10 temporary Hit Points, a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saves and a –2 item penalty to Charisma-based skill checks. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.NbSnt5tgY3veX5vP]{Effect: Hard Headed}

13-15 Hair Of The Dog

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you grow thick fur that protects from cold. You ignore the effects of severe cold, and you gain resistance 5 to cold. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.nDVc77Nshkdhj3uW]{Effect: Hair Of The Dog}

16-18 Dragon Scales

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you grow scales all over your body. You gain resistance 7 to physical damage, but each time the resistance prevents damage, it decreases by 1, as the scales flake off. When the resistance reaches 0, the scales are gone and the effect ends. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.YZEtJwCtitwL302g]{Effect: Dragon Scales}

19-21 Colorful Concoction

For 1 day, your skin color changes to a random hue.

22-24 Long In The Tooth

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you grows fangs. You gain a fangs unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage and has the finesse trait. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.80VpeFg5biDBNE2q]{Effect: Long In The Tooth}

25-27 Supersize Me

For [[/r 1d20 #minutes]]{1d20 minutes}, you become larger. You gain the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.wzctak6BxOW8xvFV]{Enlarge}. This is a polymorph effect rather than a morph effect.

28-30 Plants On The Ground

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, vegetation grows rapidly along the path you walk. The vegetation is not enough to create difficult terrain, and in most cases, it rapidly withers away after the hour is over. However, if the conditions are right, it’s possible for some of the vegetation to survive.

31-33 Putting Down Roots

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you grow roots that burrow into the floor, preventing you from moving. You are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for the duration of the effect.

34-36 I Wood If I Could

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, your skin transforms into wood. You gain the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.YWrfKetOqDwVFut7]{Barkskin}.

37-39 Love It Or Leaf It

For 1 day, your hair turns to leaves. You gain sustenance via photosynthesis and water, rather than food and water.

40-42 Overdressed

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, worn items become uncomfortable. You can’t don or wear any worn items during that time. Additionally, you must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:20] save immediately and every 10 minutes during the duration. On a failure, you must remove one of your worn items.

43-45 Earworm Supreme

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, a favorite tune repeats in your head, distracting you, providing a –2 item penalty to Perception checks and whenever you attempt an action with the concentrate trait, you must attempt a @Check[type:flat|dc:6]; on a failure, the action is disrupted. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.Y60gx7MnjRj0M4gk]{Effect: Earworm Supreme}

46-48 Something Fishy In This

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you grow gills and gain the ability to breathe water.

49-51 Beer Goggles

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} toward a random person you can see. If your fascination is broken by violence, it returns again after 1 round without violence.

52-54 Forgotten Friends

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you have a vivid hallucination that one random person you can see is an important acquaintance from your past.

55-57 Encyclopedia Intoxica

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you become trained in one random Lore skill and gain a +3 item bonus to that Lore skill, but you are compelled to talk about that topic incessantly.

58-60 Drunken Singer

For [[/r 1d4 #Hours]]{1d4 hours}, you become trained in Performance and gain a +2 item bonus to Performance checks to sing. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.H5163mWnzGCQwT3G]{Effect: Drunken Singer}

61-63 Do You Speak My Language?

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you are forced to speak a language randomly chosen by the GM instead of any language you know.

64-66 Stings The Nostrils

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, your sweat creates severe body odor. A creature that comes within 20 feet of you must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for the same duration on a critical failure). On a success, the creature is temporarily immune to body odor for 1 minute, at least under normal circumstances. However, when you enter a stressful situation, such as combat, you begin to sweat more. Creatures who had previously been temporarily immune to your body odor are no longer temporarily immune.

67-69 Fizzy Lifting Drinks

For [[/r 1d20 #Minutes]]{1d20 minutes}, you float up into the air 10 to 20 feet and hover, but you can’t control how high you are floating and have no way to move laterally, so you just float in place. This is similar to the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.fI20AVwOzJMHXRdo]{Levitate} spell except you can’t change your vertical position voluntarily.

70-72 What A Lightweight

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, your weight decreases to 1/10th of your normal weight. This doesn’t affect your size or any of your features; you simply become so low in density that you weigh much less. Your gear is unaffected. For a typical Medium creature, this reduces your Bulk, not counting your gear, to 1.

73-75 In Vino Veritas

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you can’t lie, with the effects of the cursed item ring of truth. This not only prevents deliberate lies, but also forces you to avoid lies of omission or misleading wording unless you succeed at a @Check[type:flat|dc:11].

76-78 Get Your Motor Running

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, your metabolism is supercharged. You are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened}, and you can only use the action to Strike, Stride, or Step.

79-81 The Home Stretch

For [[/r 1d20 #minutes]]{1d20 minutes}, all of your limbs elongate and your body becomes elastic. This increases your reach to 10 feet (or 5 feet if you are Tiny). This is a polymorph effect, rather than a morph effect.

82-84 I Can Shpeak Jusht Fine

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, your speech is drunkenly muddled, making it difficult to communicate, even though you’re convinced you’re talking normally. If you attempt to use an action with the linguistic trait or to cast a spell with a verbal component, you must succeed at a @Check[type:flat|dc:6] or the action or spell is disrupted. For actions that take more than 1 round, you make the flat check only after you’ve spent the time to attempt the action, as you’re @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.ds8b0pQmSvBglLVB]{Effect: I Can Shpeak Jusht Fine}convinced you were talking normally whether or not you actually were.

85-87 Punch Drunk

Your fists grow hard as wood. For 1 hour, your fists deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage and lose the nonlethal trait. During that time, your fists also become a +1 striking unarmed attack, causing them to add a +1 item bonus on attack rolls and deal a second damage die of damage. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.L6YTMVkTWTlvfVBc]{Effect: Punch Drunk}

88-90 Dizzy

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you can’t tell which direction is which. You’re @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}, and whenever you attempt to move, the GM rolls randomly to see which direction you move instead (in an open space, d8 can be used to determine whether you move NW, N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, or W, but in enclosed areas, there may be a different number of options).

91-93 Mean Drunk

You immediately enter a frenzy, which lasts for 1 minute. During this time, you gain a +4 status bonus to Strength-based damage rolls, but you attack other creatures and significant objects nearby, starting with actually life-threatening foes, moving onto those who angered or annoyed you, and saving those you cherish for last. If you would be forced to attack one of your own possessions, or a friend or close ally, you can attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:20] save to end the frenzy early. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.oknX3OCghpcxwx2f]{Effect: Mean Drunk}

94-96 Own Little World

For [[/r 10*(1d12) #Minutes]]{1d12 x 10 minutes}, you become deluded into thinking you can’t fail at anything. During that time, you experience an illusory version of reality that is the same as normal except that you succeed at everything you attempt, even if you actually failed. For example, if you fail to Force Open a door, in your mind, you actually opened it, and as everyone else sees you repeatedly walking into a closed door, you imagine yourself going inside (either using images from your memories if you know what the other side looks like or fabricating it entirely if it’s unknown to you). This applies to every check you make, so especially in combat, your opinion of how a battle is going, with you believing you hit with every attack and succeed at every saving throw, might be very different than that of everyone else.

97-99 Moment of Greatness

Somehow the Old Woody hit you with the perfect blend of effects, so when you think you’re amazing and unstoppable (as normal for alcohol), just this once it’s actually true. For 1 hour, you gain a +1 item bonus to all weapon attack rolls, unarmed attack rolls, skill checks, and saving throws. Additionally, once during that time, as a free action, you can bask in your greatness to gain a +1 status bonus to all attack rolls, skill checks, and saving throws for 1 minute. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.EDl7MrcD7SHIGqzh]{Effect: Moment of Greatness (Item Bonus)} @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-effects.VzIf5lWWO2v72T0p]{Effect: Moment of Greatness (Status Bonus)}

100 Double Whammy

Roll twice more and apply both effects, ignoring a result of 100.

Isle Tour Guides.name: 岛屿导览向导 Isle Tour Guides
Isle Tour Guides.text:
当然，我们又有什么资格来告诉你靛蓝群岛的事呢？这就是为什么我们雇了几位声名远扬的本地人，为你——读者——提供关于群岛及其千般谜团的亲身视角。每位叙述者都非常乐意与你分享他们的观点、理论、洞见与专长，因此你一定能学到这些岛屿的许多秘密！

Marrekunogriz

作为你在靛蓝群岛——尤其是金穗岛——的主要导游，请允许我先自我介绍。我是一条名为马雷库诺格里兹的小丑巨龙，但朋友们都叫我“微光”，因为我敏锐的才智能在他人身上看见伟大，即使那仅仅是未来潜能的一点微光。你也可以叫我微光；等你征服了我挚爱的群岛之谜时，我们必将成为莫逆之交。

L'orla

作为蓝铃岛的首席商人兼最负盛名的行商，我踏遍了蓝铃岛上的每一条大道、小径与羊肠小道——脚上的水泡就是证据！如果你想买些小玩意儿、工具或零嘴，我就是你要找的人。要是你有宝藏要卖，我能告诉你该去哪里出手。若你只想要一张床和一顿好晚饭，那就让我当你的向导。我对蓝铃岛以及它众多城镇与村落的熟悉程度，简直就像对自己蹄背上的纹路一样。

D'marti Tixima

欢迎！你也许光看外表看不出来，但我走遍了群岛各处：从触手河的源头一直到最远的鹿弹岛，再折返回来。我以冒险者为业，在我的时代里赚过一两次大钱，也赔过一两次大钱。你运气不错：我眼下正处在“财富空窗期”，所以才能为你奉上这趟精彩的旅行——造访金穗岛的所有聚居地，并由一位老练探险家提供无价的点评。

Zanaya

这里是扎娜娅，你可以称我为各种话题的本地专家。我大半辈子都住在隆普兰克，并以自己熟知每一条街道、每一家店铺、每一条小巷为傲，就像那是我家一样。只要是在隆普兰克发生的事，我都知道！过去几个月里，我一直在整理这份隆普兰克指南，帮助大家更了解我们这座伟大的城市。让我把你会遇见的人与地方都讲给你听。

Pelborii Karnihar

这里是佩尔博莉·卡尔尼哈尔！我是一位31岁的g'mayun女性，兴趣在情报、秘术，以及世界大事背后更深层的原因——那些有权势的人有时会试图阻止我们查明真相。哦，还有在海滩上长途散步，不过前提是海滩得在一条地脉线上，而且星辰排列得当。我是海景城议会议员，代表非orpok群体的利益，所以关于这座城市我有很多话要说。还有，别忘了在即将到来的议会选举中投我一票！

Elder Dyna

长者戴娜是一位来自更早年代的著名说书人，最初出生于森巴拉城，远离靛蓝群岛。他是平衡万神殿的祭司，同时同等崇敬父神托瓦与母神莉琳。戴娜所讲述的平衡创世神话，恰好被在场之人记录成文。如今，它已被译成80多种语言，并成为流传最广的前五之列，不过其中某些段落仍颇具争议。

Kallizandrex

我是卡利赞德雷克斯，杰出的绿巨龙学者；你或许听说过我在巨龙研究与lux aeterna仪式方面无与伦比的学术成就。别担心；你尽可放心，我会用连一只绿巨龙雏龙都能听懂的方式解释一切，因此希望你们其他人也能听懂。也许外面的青铜巨龙除外……当然，我是在开玩笑。又或者我并没有！

An'zoki

你是被我的鼓声吸引到我这儿来的，对吧？我就知道！好啦，你既然来了！而且你看起来正是那种会想了解靛蓝群岛中大多数人宁愿忘掉的地方的人——那座只有我，失信定位者安佐奇，才敢唱出其歌的岛屿。我说的当然是那座被称作月影岛的闹鬼之岛！来，围过来，我给你讲讲这段故事。

Captain Nupor Stormbar

打起精神来，你这没胆子的空中住民！你也许在桶赛跑上证明了自己的本事——我承认，你的表演不错——但想打动努波尔·风杠船长，可不止这点能耐！所以你想知道关于礁区的事，是吧？还有那些空中住民没法告诉你的其他岛屿——因为他们压根没听说过？我还不确定我想不想告诉你，但你确实找对地方了。

Of course, who are we to tell you about the Indigo Isles? That's why we hired several renowned locals to provide you, the reader, with a personal perspective of the Isles and all their varied mysteries. Each narrator is all too happy to share with you their views, theories, insights, and expertise, so you're sure to learn many of the islands' secrets!

Marrekunogriz

As your primary tour guide around the Indigo Isles, specifically Goldcrop, do allow me to introduce myself. I am a Harlequin Dragon named Marrekunogriz, but my friends call me Glimmer because of the way that my acute intellect sees greatness in others, even when it is only a glimmer of potential in the future. You can call me Glimmer; we shall be great friends by the time you've conquered the mysteries of my beloved Isles.

L'orla

As Bluebell's master merchant and preeminent peddler, I've tramped down every road, trail, and footpath in Bluebell, and I have the blisters to prove it! If you're looking to buy trinkets, tools, or treats, I'm your woman. If you've got treasure to sell, I can tell you where to unload it. If all you want is a bed and a good dinner, let me be your guide to the island. I know Bluebell and its many towns and villages like the back of my hoof.

D'marti Tixima

Welcome! You might not be able to tell just by looking at me, but I've traveled all over the Isles: from the source of the Tentacle River all the way to the farthest Buckshot Isle and back. I'm an adventurer by trade, and I've made and lost a fortune or two in my time. You're in luck: I'm between fortunes at the moment, which is why I'm able to offer you this amazing tour—a visit to all of Goldcrop's settlements, accompanied by the invaluable commentary of a seasoned explorer.

Zanaya

Zanaya here, and you can call me a local expert on all sorts of topics. I've lived most of my life in Rumplank, and I pride myself on knowing every street, storefront, and back alley, like they were my home. If it happened in Rumplank, I know about it! I've been putting together this guide to Rumplank over the past few months to help folks learn more about our great city. Let me tell you all about the people and places you'll find.

Pelborii Karnihar

Pelborii Karnihar here! I'm a 31-year-old g'mayun woman with interests in information, the mystic arts, and the deeper causes behind world events that powerful folk sometimes try to stop us from finding out. Oh, and long walks on the beach, but only as long as it's on a ley line and the stars are aligned. I'm a member of the Seaview city council, representing non-orpok interests, so I have a lot to say about the city. And don't forget to vote for me in the upcoming council election!

Elder Dyna

Elder Dyna is a renowned storyteller from an earlier age, originally born in the city of Sembala, far from the Indigo Isles. A priest of the Balance pantheon worshipping both Tova the Father and Lilin the Mother equally, Dyna's version of the Balance creation myth happened to be transcribed by someone in attendance. Today it's been translated into over 80 languages and has became one of the top five most prevalent, while certain passages remain contentious.

Kallizandrex

I am Kallizandrex, green dragon scholar extraordinaire; you might have heard of my peerless scholarship on dragons and the lux aeterna ritual. Don't worry; you can rest assured that I'll explain everything in a way that even a hatchling green dragon can understand, and therefore hopefully, so can the rest of you. Except maybe the bronze dragons out there… though I am jesting, of course. Or maybe I'm not!

An'zoki

You were drawn over here to me by my drumming, yes? I knew it! Well, you're here now! And you certainly look like the type who would love to learn about the one place in the Indigo Isles that most folk would rather forget—the one isle whose song only I, An'zoki the Locator of Lost Letters, will dare to sing. I speak, of course, about the haunted isle known as Moonshadow Island! Come, gather ‘round, and I'll tell you the tale.

Captain Nupor Stormbar

Look alive, you lily-livered airdweller! You might have proved your talent for barrel racing—you put on a good show, I'll admit—but it takes more than that to impress Captain Nupor Stormbar! So you want to know about the reef, do you? And all the other islands the airdwellers can't tell you about—on account of they haven't heard of them? I don't know if I want to tell you, but you've come to the right place.

Ancestries of the Isles.name: 群岛的族裔 Ancestries of the Isles
Ancestries of the Isles.text:
马雷库诺格里兹关于群岛上诸多面貌的笔记。

承认吧。你从大陆来到这里时，最先注意到的大概就是这里的居民和你以往习惯见到的人有点不一样。也许你在扬帆出航前就听说过，但毕竟“眼见为实”，书本或口头传说与亲眼在面前经历真实的一切之间，总有些不同，对吧。

在靛蓝群岛，每个家族在过去某个时点都曾像你一样：新来的，满怀惊奇与兴奋……也许陆地上的某些克拉格拉克和浪下的乔科里除外，不过即便如此，我们也并不确定。

如今，你几乎在每个聚落都能看到各种族的人混居协作，运用各自独特的能力与视角，为共同利益服务。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.AsvbXFGEiyro3tps]{G'mayuns} 是群岛上人口最多的族群之一；而他们那一身五彩羽毛与彩虹般的眼睛，即便在这里也格外显眼。你现在肯定已经见过一些了吧？他们看起来有点像小型的双足鹦鹉。由于“镀金海盗”波比·冯·藤壶的名声，以及统治朗普兰克的王室红羽家族，g'mayun 在群岛历史中赢得了重要地位。有些 g'mayun 甚至会自称是这里最具代表性的居民，不过在我看来，那股味道挺像自负。g'mayun 也许显得乐天，但他们身上牵扯的事可不少——他们的历史涉及恶魔试图腐化他们彼此共享的一首神秘之歌。我建议你别在 g'mayun 面前提这事，除非你已经准备好承担各种可能的后果：从被上课式长篇讲解，到被冷淡对待，甚至来一段由焦虑驱动的独白。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.nICkhRGYP09AX4Sb]{Kragraks} 就是你见过的那种看起来有点像岩石、沙砾、火山之类东西的人。他们在这里存在的时间最久，而且正如你所料，他们与大地的元素力量有着深刻共鸣；此外，每个克拉格拉克还会有某种花哨的次级元素，因人而异。选择、变化与不对称对克拉格拉克相当重要；他们一生会经历若干仪式来选择自己的道路，甚至包括决定自己看起来像灰烬、巨砾或别的什么。所有这些选择都各有含义，但其个人含义也会因克拉格拉克而异，所以别以为你能自己把一切都看明白。哦，再给你一句忠告：他们把元素分成几种类型；要是他们把大地也算作其中一种类型，你可能会以为每种类型都会有五个，但他们不这么算。他们认为大地超越了他们的分类，因此产生了一种美丽的不对称。有太多人了解这套说法后跑去跟他们提这点，现在他们已经被烦透了；如果我是你，就会避开这个话题。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.nj42RSFQx6dKl8aW]{Hardriggans}——那些长着公羊角与特性的家伙——依靠自身的力量与能力生存，带着一点“强者即正义”的态度，既可能造就恶霸，也可能造就英雄，甚至更多。他们独特的社会结构称为“柱（Columns）”，反映了他们对力量阶层动态的迷恋：柱顶的最强者要求下方的哈德里甘缴纳贡赋并宣誓效忠，作为交换，他们会借出自己的力量来保护更弱的哈德里甘。若你让某个哈德里甘谈起他们的柱，准备好听一段很长的对话吧。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.dOxlZHOcFKvob6AL]{Chochori} 在群岛上可能不那么常见，但那是因为他们大多生活在附近的海洋里。他们看起来像是由珊瑚构成——因为他们就是珊瑚。和珊瑚一样，他们的生物学与我们其他人大不相同，这有时会导致误解。他们已经受够了我们把他们的七种 chori 当作类似“性别”的东西，所以吸取过去与乔科里打过交道者的教训，千万别做这种类比。要我说，他们的 chori 更像是人格类型或社会角色，而且还会随着时间发生变化！

在靛蓝群岛各处都能找到小妖精（特征），而虽然你可能在大陆上就见过这些小仙灵，但你也许没听过这里小妖精（特征）中最常见的那种 @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-heritages.Item.ZpmfVnjRoL2scN2F]{galtzagorri} 传承。他们热爱建造，而且确实很擅长。某种程度上，他们与勤勉的 melixies 相处得来，但双方的态度完全不同。留心他们标志性的瓢虫翅膀与钩长矛！

一个充满原始能量与 vitae（有时也被称作自然灵体）的地方同时也是许多莱西的家园，这大概并不令人意外。只要有德鲁伊能将这些 vitae 具现为植物形态，就会有莱西存在；而我从未听说过哪里有比这里更多的莱西。群岛拥有无数生态环境，每种都有自己的莱西；此外，还有一种在此地常见、但在别处很少见的独特莱西传承：@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-heritages.Item.Ut588yPvW1A2oe56]{wildfire leshies}——其形态代表野火所带来的复苏性相。我听说最早让他们在群岛各处繁衍开来的，是一个由克拉格拉克德鲁伊组成的古老教团；老实说，如果这是真的，我一点也不惊讶。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.MxcoZ79OYNIxUZnK]{Orpoks}——那些看起来像类人生物猪的家伙——是群岛的新来者，不过他们在风铃群岛的海景城带来了很大影响。若你相信那些最离奇的边缘奥尔波克历史学家的故事，他们甚至可能在远古时代就曾出现在群岛上。这确实是个相当有可能的情况：只要把时代往前翻上几百年左右，群岛的历史就会变得异常模糊且充满矛盾。无论他们是否是最先抵达群岛的奥尔波克，这些奥尔波克都是从一支庞大舰队中分出的几艘船抵达此地——那支舰队在海上漂泊数十年寻找新家园，每艘船都承载着他们社会历史与文化的不同碎片。长话短说，成功抵达这里的船队带来了他们几乎全部关于饮食文化的知识以及少量其他内容，却没带来多少别的东西，从而形成了一种极端丰富、但又失衡的饮食文化。

小妖精（特征）并非群岛上唯一的妖精族裔。@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.wFxve7CYmBUjAm2c]{Bacoo} 源自一群小型变形者的后裔；他们曾作为难民来到这里，不过如今他们只会以特定方式改变。结果是，笑话落在他们自己身上——我们的世界比他们离开的那个还要残酷。即便如此，无论是出于倔强还是别无选择，他们仍留在此地，恶作剧、开派对，并且总体上彼此抱团，同时对其他族裔保持警惕。他们不轻易信任，但一旦信任你，就会信任你一辈子。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.vgvOghxMwyXxuiO7]{Bannog} 就是你在这附近见过的兔人。虽然他们并非来自妖精之地，但他们来自梦境之地！鉴于其起源，他们认为自己在睡眠与做梦中度过的那段人生，与清醒在外活动的那段同样重要（或者对他们中的一些人而言，更重要），也就不奇怪了。别吓坏了——听起来没那么糟——但他们能啃食灵魂。是的，真的。不过他们并非必须如此，而且也不像外头那些其他噬魂者。这也是他们来到这里的原因。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.dEIrFX5xBcdJVeKh]{Xotlxotl} 是那种蝾螈类型的家伙——你可能注意到你第一次见到他们时，他们对你特别友好、特别乐于帮忙，对吧？是的，我就知道。那表示他们不喜欢你。在他们的文化里，对一个人严厉意味着你在乎对方、希望对方成长与进步；而帮助对方则表示你希望对方一直软弱无能。我打赌你没想到吧！

至于我——马雷库诺格里兹，你在靛蓝群岛的卑微向导——怎么样？嗯，我们今天不聊我；但如果你非要知道，那是的：群岛上有很多巨龙，包括像我这样的永恒巨龙，生活在其他族裔之中。

Marrekunogriz's notes on the many faces found on the Isles.

Admit it. The first thing you probably noticed when you arrived here from the mainland is that the people living here are a little different than what you might be used to seeing. Maybe you heard about it before you set sail, but they say that seeing is believing, after all, and there's just something different between a book or spoken tale and experiencing the real thing, right in front of your eyes.

Here in the Indigo Isles, every family was like you at some point in the past: new arrivals, full of wonder and excitement... except maybe some kragrak on the land and chochori beneath the waves, although even then, we're not so sure.

Nowadays, you'll see a mix of people in just about every settlement working together, using their own unique abilities and perspectives to their collective advantage.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.AsvbXFGEiyro3tps]{G'mayuns} are among the most populous of all people here on the Isles and certainly stand out, even here, due to all the colorful plumage and rainbow eyes. Surely you've seen some by now? They're the folks who look a bit like small bipedal parrots. Thanks to the fame of Poppy von Barnacle, the Gilded Pirate, and the royal Redfeathers who rule over Rumplank, g'mayun have earned an important place in the history of the Isles. Some g'mayun might even call themselves the most iconic of the inhabitants here, though to me, that reeks of hubris. G'mayuns might seem happy-go-lucky, but they have a lot going on with a history involving demons trying to corrupt a mystic song that they share between them. I'd say don't bring it up in g'mayun company unless you're ready for the possible outcomes, which range from a lecture to the cold shoulder or even an angst-fueled soliloquy.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.nICkhRGYP09AX4Sb]{Kragraks} are the people you've been seeing who look a bit like rocks, sand, volcanoes, and those sorts of things. They've been around here the longest, and they're deeply in tune with the elemental power of earth, as you might expect, as well as some sort of fancy secondary element that varies from kragrak to kragrak. Choice, change, and asymmetry are pretty important to kragraks, and they go through a few rituals throughout their lives to choose their path, even including whether they look like ash, boulders, or something else. All of those choices have meanings, but they might have different personal meanings that vary from kragrak to kragrak, so don't go thinking you can figure it all out for yourself. Oh, and a word of advice: they split up the elements into a few types, and you would think there would be five of each if they counted earth as being one of those types, but they don't. They think of earth as transcending their classification, thus resulting in a beautiful asymmetry. Way too many people learn about this whole thing and mention this fact to them, and now it just annoys them, so I would steer clear of the topic if I were you.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.nj42RSFQx6dKl8aW]{Hardriggans}, the folks with ram horns and features, live by their own power and ability, with a bit of a might makes right attitude that can lead to bullies and heroes both, and more besides. Their unique social structures, called Columns, reflect this fixation on the dynamics of power hierarchies, with the most powerful at the top of the Column, demanding tribute and fealty in exchange for lending their strength to protect the weaker hardriggans below. If you start a hardriggan talking about their Column, expect a long conversation.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.dOxlZHOcFKvob6AL]{Chochori} might be a bit less common on the Isles, but that's because most of them live in the nearby oceans. They look like they're made of coral, but that's because they are. Just like coral, their biology is pretty different from the rest of us, and that can sometimes lead to misunderstandings. They are absolutely sick of the rest of us thinking that their seven chori are like genders, so learn from the mistakes of those who've dealt with chochori in the past and do not try to make that analogy. If anything, I feel like their chori are more like personality types or societal roles, which can shift over time!

You can find sprites all over the Indigo Isles, and while the little fairies might be familiar from the mainland, you might not have heard of the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-heritages.Item.ZpmfVnjRoL2scN2F]{galtzagorri} heritage that predominates the sprites seen here. They love to build things, and they're quite good at it. In a way, they get along with industrious melixies, but their attitudes are completely different. Look out for their telltale ladybug wings and hook spears!

It's probably not a huge surprise that a place as rife with primal energies and vitae (sometimes called nature spirits) is also home to many leshies. As long as there are druids who can embody those vitae into plant forms, there will be leshies around, and I've never heard of a place with more of them than there are here. The Isles are home to countless biomes, each with their own leshies, as well as a distinctive heritage of leshies found commonly here but rarely anywhere else. These are @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-heritages.Item.Ut588yPvW1A2oe56]{wildfire leshies}, leshies whose forms represent the rejuvenating aspect of a wildfire. I've heard that the ones who first proliferated them throughout the Isles were an ancient circle of kragrak druids, and honestly, if that's true, it wouldn't surprise me at all.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.MxcoZ79OYNIxUZnK]{Orpoks}, the folks who look like humanoid pigs, are newcomers to the Isles, though they've made a big impact with the city of Seaview on Bluebell Isles. And if you believe the wildest tales of fringe orpok historians, they might have been present on the Isles in the ancient past. That's honestly a distinct possibility, as the history of the Isles becomes notably fuzzy and rife with contradictions once you page back a few centuries or so. Whether or not they're the first orpoks to reach the Isles, these orpoks arrived as a few ships from a massive fleet that sailed the seas for decades seeking a new home, each holding different pieces of their society's history and culture. Long story short, the ships that made it here included just about everything they knew about food culture and a few other things, but not much else, leading to a lopsidedly rich food culture.

Sprites aren't the only fey ancestry in the Isles. @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.wFxve7CYmBUjAm2c]{Bacoo} descend from minor shapeshifters who traveled here as refugees, though nowadays they only change in specific ways. It turns out, the joke was on them, and our world was an even harsher place than the one they left to come here. Even so, whether through stubbornness or lack of choice, they stuck around, playing pranks, throwing parties, and generally sticking together with each other while being wary of other ancestries. They don't trust quickly, but if they trust you, they trust you for life.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.vgvOghxMwyXxuiO7]{Bannog} are those rabbit people you've seen around here, and while they aren't from the fey lands, they do come from the land of dreams! Given their origin, it's probably not a big surprise that they consider the part of their lives they spend asleep and dreaming to be equally important to the part they spend awake and about (or, to some of them, more important). Don't freak out, since it's really not as bad as it sounds, but they can nibble on souls. Yes, really. But they don't have to, and they're not like other soul eaters out there either. That's why they've come here.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-ancestries.Item.dEIrFX5xBcdJVeKh]{Xotlxotl} are those salamander types who you might have noticed were really friendly and helpful to you when you first met, right? Yep, I figured. Means they don't like you. In their culture, being harsh on someone means you care about them and want them to grow and improve, while helping them out means you want them to stay weak. I bet you didn't expect that!

What about me, Marrekunogriz, your humble tour guide around the Indigo Isles? Well, we're not talking about me today, but if you must know, then yes, there are plenty of dragons here in the Isles, including aeternal dragons just like me who live among the other ancestries.

The Isles.name: 群岛 The Isles
The Isles.text:
作为你在靛蓝群岛——尤其是金穗岛——的主要导游，请允许我先自我介绍。我是一条名叫马雷库诺格里兹的小丑巨龙，但我的朋友们叫我“微光”，因为我敏锐的才智总能在他人身上看见伟大——即使那只是未来潜力的一缕微光。你也可以叫我微光；等你征服了我挚爱的群岛之谜时，我们必将成为最要好的朋友。

马雷库诺格里兹关于最大靛蓝岛的独家内幕：@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.hEW9VvPdJXU1YVdd]{Goldcrop Isle}

作为风铃岛的首席商人兼首屈一指的行商，我踏遍了风铃岛的每一条道路、小径与羊肠小道，脚上的水泡就是证据！如果你想买小玩意儿、工具或甜点，我就是你要找的人。要是你有宝藏要卖，我也能告诉你去哪儿出手。若你只想要一张床和一顿丰盛的晚餐，就让我来做你的向导。我对风铃岛以及它众多的城镇与村落熟得就像自己的蹄背一样。

L'orla的旅行指南，带你走进群岛的粮仓：@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.aef8bdHrOuG4eyx1]{Bluebell Isle}

你是被我的鼓声吸引到我这儿来的，对吧？我就知道！好啦，你既然来了！而且你看起来正是那种会想了解靛蓝群岛里大多数人宁愿忘掉的地方的人——那座只有我，“迷失信件的定位者”安佐基，才敢歌唱其曲的岛。我说的当然是那座被称为月影岛的闹鬼之岛！来吧，围过来，我会把这个故事讲给你听。

安佐基对拉金·普伦德发现的日志的复述：@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.viSocpLw8O1QSa8x]{Moonshadow Isle}

打起精神来，你这胆小的空居者！你也许在桶赛中证明了自己的本事——我承认，你的表演确实不错——但光凭那点可别想打动努珀·暴风坝船长！所以你想知道暗礁的事，是吗？还有其他那些空居者没法告诉你的岛——毕竟他们连听都没听过？我也不确定我想不想说，不过你确实来对地方了。

努珀·暴风坝船长的海之感知：@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.9T0ckPDL1v6Tg9dM]{Runaway Reef & Other Islands}

As your primary tour guide around the Indigo Isles, specifically Goldcrop, do allow me to introduce myself. I am a Harlequin Dragon named Marrekunogriz, but my friends call me Glimmer because of the way that my acute intellect sees greatness in others, even when it is only a glimmer of potential in the future. You can call me Glimmer; we shall be great friends by the time you've conquered the mysteries of my beloved Isles.

Marrekunogriz's inside scoop on the largest Indigo Isle: @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.hEW9VvPdJXU1YVdd]{Goldcrop Isle}

As Bluebell's master merchant and preeminent peddler, I've tramped down every road, trail, and footpath in Bluebell, and I have the blisters to prove it! If you're looking to buy trinkets, tools, or treats, I'm your woman. If you've got treasure to sell, I can tell you where to unload it. If all you want is a bed and a good dinner, let me be your guide to the island. I know Bluebell and its many towns and villages like the back of my hoof.

L'orla's travel guide to the breadbasket of the Isles: @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.aef8bdHrOuG4eyx1]{Bluebell Isle}

You were drawn over here to me by my drumming, yes? I knew it! Well, you're here now! And you certainly look like the type who would love to learn about the one place in the Indigo Isles that most folk would rather forget—the one isle whose song only I, An'zoki the Locator of Lost Letters, will dare to sing. I speak, of course, about the haunted isle known as Moonshadow Island! Come, gather ‘round, and I'll tell you the tale.

An'zoki's retelling of Ruggin Proonder's discovered journal: @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.viSocpLw8O1QSa8x]{Moonshadow Isle}

Look alive, you lily-livered airdweller! You might have proved your talent for barrel racing—you put on a good show, I'll admit—but it takes more than that to impress Captain Nupor Stormbar! So you want to know about the reef, do you? And all the other islands the airdwellers can't tell you about—on account of they haven't heard of them? I don't know if I want to tell you, but you've come to the right place.

Captain Nupor Stormbar's wisdom of the sea: @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-journals.JournalEntry.9T0ckPDL1v6Tg9dM]{Runaway Reef & Other Islands}

1. 定义：(a) “贡献者（Contributors）”指贡献了开放游戏内容（Open Game Content）的版权和/或商标所有人；(b) “衍生材料（Derivative Material）”指受版权保护的材料，包括衍生作品与翻译（包括翻译为其他计算机语言），誊写、修改、校正、增补、扩展、升级、改进、汇编、节略或任何其他形式，使既有作品得以被改写、转换或改编；(c) “分发（Distribute）”指复制、许可、出租、租赁、出售、广播、公开展示、传输或以其他方式分发；(d) “开放游戏内容（Open Game Content）”指游戏机制，并包括方法、程序、流程与例程，在该等内容不体现产品标识（Product Identity）的范围内，且相较既有技术具有改进，并包括由贡献者明确标识为开放游戏内容的任何附加内容；并指任何受本许可覆盖的作品，包括依据版权法的翻译与衍生作品，但明确排除产品标识。(e) “产品标识（Product Identity）”指产品与产品线名称、标识与识别标记（包括商业外观）；神器、生物、角色、故事、故事线、情节、主题元素、对话、事件、语言、艺术作品、符号、设计、描绘、肖像、格式、姿势、概念、主题以及图形、摄影与其他视觉或音频表现；角色、法术、附魔、人格、团队、化身、肖像与特殊能力的名称与描述；地点、位置、环境、生物、装备、魔法或超自然能力或效果、标识、符号或图形设计；以及由产品标识所有者明确标识为产品标识的任何其他商标或注册商标；并明确排除开放游戏内容。(f) “商标（Trademark）”指由贡献者用于识别其自身或其产品，或用于识别由贡献者贡献至开放游戏许可的相关产品的标识、名称、标记、符号、口号、设计。(g) “使用（Use）”“已使用（Used）”或“正在使用（Using）”指使用、分发、复制、编辑、排版、修改、翻译以及以其他方式基于开放游戏内容创造衍生材料。(h) “你（You）”或“你的（Your）”指本协议项下的被许可人。

2. 本许可：本许可适用于任何包含通知、表明该开放游戏内容仅可依照本许可并按本许可条款使用的开放游戏内容。你必须将该等通知附加至你所使用的任何开放游戏内容。除本许可自身所述情形外，不得对本许可增加或删减任何条款。不得对使用本许可分发的任何开放游戏内容适用任何其他条款或条件。

3. 要约与承诺：通过使用开放游戏内容，你表明你接受本许可条款。

4. 授权与对价：作为同意使用本许可之对价，贡献者授予你一项永久、全球范围、免版税、非排他性的许可，且其条款与本许可完全一致，以使用开放游戏内容。

5. 贡献授权之声明：若你以开放游戏内容形式贡献原创材料，你声明你的贡献为你的原创创造和/或你拥有足够权利授予本许可所传递之权利。

6. 许可版权通知：你必须更新本许可中的 COPYRIGHT NOTICE（版权通知）部分，以包含你正在复制、修改或分发的任何开放游戏内容之 COPYRIGHT NOTICE 的完整文本；并且你必须将标题、版权日期以及版权持有人姓名添加至你所分发的任何原创开放游戏内容之 COPYRIGHT NOTICE 中。

7. 产品标识之使用：你同意不使用任何产品标识，包括作为兼容性之指示，除非在与各该产品标识要素之所有者另行订立的、独立的协议中获得明确许可。你同意，除非在与该商标或注册商标之所有者另行订立的、独立的协议中获得明确许可，否则不得在包含开放游戏内容的作品中，结合任何商标或注册商标指示兼容性或可共同改编性。在开放游戏内容中使用任何产品标识不构成对该产品标识所有权的挑战。任何在开放游戏内容中使用的产品标识之所有者应保留该产品标识的全部权利、所有权与利益。

8. 标识：若你分发开放游戏内容，你必须清楚标明你所分发作品中的哪些部分属于开放游戏内容。

9. 更新许可：Wizards 或其指定代理人可发布本许可的更新版本。你可以使用任何经授权的本许可版本来复制、修改与分发最初在本许可任何版本下分发的任何开放游戏内容。

10. 本许可副本：你必须在你分发的开放游戏内容的每一份副本中包含本许可的一份副本。

11. 贡献者鸣谢之使用：除非你已获得贡献者的书面许可，否则你不得使用任何贡献者的姓名来营销或广告开放游戏内容。

12. 无法遵守：若由于成文法、司法命令或政府规章，导致你就部分或全部开放游戏内容无法遵守本许可的任何条款，则你不得使用任何受该等影响的开放游戏材料。

13. 终止：若你未能遵守本许可所载全部条款，且在知悉违约之日起 30 日内未纠正该违约，本许可将自动终止。所有分许可在本许可终止后仍继续有效。

14. 修改：若本许可的任何条款被认定为不可执行，则该条款仅应在使其可执行所必需的范围内予以修改。
